Title: JAMA patient page. Recognizing measles.
Abstract: Measles is a highly contagious disease that is caused by a virus. How Measles Is Spread Measles is spread when an infected person coughs or sneezes. Small droplets containing the virus disperse into the air and onto nearby surfaces, where the virus can live for up to 2 hours. Another person can become infected by breathing contaminated air or through contact with a contaminated surface. A person who is infected with measles can spread the disease to others even before any symptoms appear. Measles is one of the most contagious diseases known. This means that it spreads easily from an infected person to others who are not immune to the disease. People who are fully vaccinated against measles, who have had the disease, or who were born in the United States before 1957 are considered immune.

Title: Analysis of IgM determination in 157 suspected measles in Minhang district of Shanghai
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Health Laboratory Technology
Abstract: Objective:To explore the method of improving accuracy rate of the anti-measles IgM detection and the epidemiological characteristics of measles in Minhang district.Methods: Blood specimens were collected and tested for anti-measles IgM by ELISA.Results: There were 157 cases included in this study.Those with anti-measles IgM positive were found in 108 measles cases,accounting for 68.8%.The cases dispersed all the year round,the incidence peaked in July,and high-incidence season is from April to Sepertember.The difference of measles infection rate among age groups was significant(P0.01),and the positive cases mainly distributed in age group under 1.5 years old and above 18 years old.The difference of measles infection rate between natives and foreign population was also significant(P0.05).Conclusion: The accuracy of IgM detection mainly depends on the symptom and sampling instant.The measles showed distinct seasonal characteristic.Uninoculation was mainly responsible for measles.Basic immunization and immunological surveillance of measles should be implemented to control and eliminate measles.

Title: Progress in reducing global measles deaths, 1999-2004.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: Measles remains a substantial cause of global childhood mortality, particularly in developing countries. In their joint strategic plan for Measles Mortality Reduction, 2001-2005, the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) targeted 45 priority countries with the highest measles mortality for implementation of a comprehensive strategy for accelerated and sustained measles mortality reduction. Components of this strategy include achieving high routine vaccination coverage (>90%) in every district and ensuring that all children receive a second opportunity for measles vaccination. In May 2003, the World Health Assembly endorsed a resolution urging member countries to achieve a goal (adopted in 2002 by the United Nations General Assembly Special Session on Children) to reduce 1999 deaths resulting from measles by half by the end of 2005. This report updates progress toward this goal and introduces a new goal for measles mortality reduction by 2010.
MeSH Terms: Global Health; Humans; Immunization Programs; Measles/mortality; Measles Vaccine/administration & dosage

Title: Isolation of Wild Type Measles Virus and its Influence Factors
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Public Health Engineering
Abstract: Objective To determine the genotype of measles virus which circulated in Jilin province within recent years,analyze the influence factors on measles virus isolation for setting down the measures of prevention and control and to provide the reference basis on the pathogenic detection of measles.Methods The Vero/Slam cell were used to isolate the measles virus on the 114 swab samples and the RT-PCR-RFLP method was used to identify the nucleic acid of measles virus and genotype of measles virus.Influence of different sampling time and different swab preserving liquids on the measles virus isolation was analyzed.Results 31 strains of measles virus were isolated from 114 swab samples and the isolation rate was higher(38.09%) of the swab samples which collected within 2 days after rash occurrence.The isolation rate of measles virus was 30.69% by 2% DMEM as swab preserving liquid and 0% by 0.9% salt as swab preserving liquid.Conclusion H1 genotype of measles virus is the main genotype which circulated in Jilin province.The successful isolation of measles virus depends on sampling time,good sample quality and right sample preserving liquid.
Fields of Study: Genotype; Virology; Wild type; Isolation (microbiology); Measles; Measles virus; Rash; Isolation rate; Sample quality; Biology; Microbiology

Title: Analysis on the Prevalent Characteristics of Measles in Henan Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: In the analysis of the prevalent characteristics of measles in Henan Province, we found that in Henan Province the measles incidence has greatly decreased along with using measles vaccine and launching EPI, and the lower incidence of 10 cases per 100,000 population remained through 1990's. Seasonal distribution of measles was very different, more cases occurred in spring and winter, especially in April. In the same time, measles incidence was dominated by children aged 1 to 9 years. The incidence was very different among prefectures, and the outbreaks still existed in some areas. So, based on concrete conditions, through implementing the plan of measles control in all prefectures, establishing or developing surveillance system, launching measles vaccine mass campaigns, and reducing outbreaks, the goal for accelerating measles control towards measles elimination will be achieved.

Title: Progress Towards Global Measles Elimination: a Summary
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: In 1989, Word Health Assembly endorsed the goal of global measles control and elimination, the main strategies of measles elimination in regions are strengthen immunization and surveillance, included increasing routine immunization coverage of measles containing vaccine, conducting high quality catch up and follow up measles supplementary immunization activities, and establishing case based measles surveillance system supported by laboratory network. All regions have established measles elimination goal except South-East Asia, in which the goal will be considered. The estimated measles death was 164 000 in 2008, reducing 78 % comparing with that in 2000, and all regions except South-east Asia have reached the goal of reducing death by 90 % . A new global goal of measles eradication will be proposed in 2010. Americas region of WHO have reached and sustained measles elimination status since 2002, providing other regions with experiences and confidence in measles elimination. However, to reach the goal of global measles elimination is facing huge challenges. High level political commitment from the government and resources are in urgent needed.
Fields of Study: Government; Immunology; Measles; Immunization; Routine immunization; Measles elimination; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Analysis on running status of Guizhou Provincial Measles Laboratory Network in 2007
Source Title: Zhongguo yi miao he mian yi
Author/s: Sheng-Wei Wu; Xu-Fang Ye; Li Zhang
Abstract: OBJECTIVE To evaluate the running status of the Guizhou provincial measles laboratory network in 2007. METHODS To analyze the testing result of measles laboratory network and data of measles Surveillance system (MSS). RESULTS 1. Serologic surveillance: 1645 suspected measles cases were reported in MSS in 2007, and 1454 measles sera samples were collected, and the collection rate was 88.4%. The measles cases were 915, among 191 were diagnosed by clinic, and 724 were confirmed by laboratory (79.1%). 27 of 28 outbreaks were laboratory diagnosed as measles. The measles IgG of 726 healthy population sera were tested, and the positive rate was 70.3%. and geometric mean titer was 1:492. 6. 2. Viruology surveillance: from 35 throat swab or urine samples collected in 2007, 11 strains of wild measles virus were isolated, all of them were H1a genotype. 3. Laboratory network quality control: the provincial Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) measles laboratory passed the proficiency test, the sera samples recheck and on-site review held by the China CDC National Measles Laboratory with good performance in 2007. Nine prefectures CDC measles laboratories passed the sera proficiency test and on-site review held by the provincial CDC. 215 sera samples were rechecked, among which, 202 result was as same as the provincial laboratory, the accordance rate was 94.0%. CONCLUSION Running status of the whole provincial measles laboratories network were good in 2007, and the good laboratory quality control system was also set up, and they play an important role in measles surveillance.

Title: Low antibody response in infants with measles and children with subclinical measles virus infection
Source Title: The Journal of tropical medicine and hygiene
Author/s: T Cherian; A Joseph; T J John
Abstract: During a measles epidemic (December 1979-March 1980) in two adjacent villages in Tamil Nadu, 78 of 143 under-fives were affected, eight of whom died, giving an attack rate of 54% and case-fatality rate of 10%. Seven months later 72 children (41 with measles, seven with history of measles in prior epidemics and 24 with no history of measles) were bled to measure measles virus haemagglutination-inhibition antibody. Of the 48 with measles history, 46 had measurable antibody. Surprisingly, of the 24 without a history, 16 had measurable antibody indicating the frequency of subclinical measles during epidemics. The geometric mean antibody titre was lower in infants with measles than in older children (P less than 0.05). The mean titre was lower in those with subclinical measles than in those with clinical measles (P less than 0.01).

Title: Epidemiology of measles in Anyang,Henan,1982-2009
Abstract: Objective To analyze the epidemiological characteristics of measles in Anyang,Henan and provide scientific evidence for improving measles prevention and control.Methods Measles surveillance data were collected from 1982 to 2009 in Anyang,Henan.A descriptive epidemiological method was used for the study.Results 29 571 measles cases were reported from 1982 to 2009 inclusive,with the average annual incidence rate of 21.23/lakh.43(0.15%)death cases were confirmed.The annual rate of measles incidences varied during the period,which had the highest in 1985.The annual incidence rate of measles declined significantly after the year of 1986,which was coincidently occurred with the implementation of vaccine cold chain system in China.Measles cases were reported in all nine districts of Anyang and May had the most of cases generally.The ratio of measles cases was 1.3∶1 for males to females.Of the cases,73.89% occurred in children under 7 years old.More than half(58.21%) measles cases were from dispersed children.Of the measles cases occurred between 2000 and 2009,only 38.62% patients had a verified vaccination history and 78.24% had positive IgM antibody.Conclusion It is important to increase the coverage rate of measles vaccination and provide catch-up immunization for dispersed children.

Title: Measles and its control: dogmas and new perspectives.
Abstract: In 1987 measles killed 2 million children a year which figure dropped to 900000 by 1991 as a result of improved immunization coverage in developing countries. The clinical and immunological features of measles involve pneumonia as a common complication leading to death diarrhea salmonella infection giant cell pneumonia without rash in children with kwashiorkor dying of measles weight loss precipitation of kwashiorkor and precipitation of blindness. In Africa popular beliefs about measles include getting the rash out by rubbing the skin with palm oil or kerosene and other practices that aggravate dehydration. The World Health Organization estimation of 2 million deaths was based on a 3.2% case fatality rate (CFR) of children. As the measles virus is considered stable differences in its virulence are unlikely to play a major role in the pathogenesis of severe measles. More important factors are: malnourishment particularly vitamin A deficiency; age at infection with a higher mortality among the youngest children especially in rural areas; preexisting diseases e.g. tuberculosis and kwashiorkor; and availability of medical care. Cross-sex transmission also resulted in higher mortality in secondary cases in Guinea-Bissau and Senegal. West Africa has the worlds highest CFR in measles on account of the highest proportion of polygynous families and large households. Delayed mortality from measles is two to three times higher among previous measles cases than among controls in West Africa. Immunity in children derives from maternal immunity or vaccine-induced immunity. Despite arguments that nutrition programs should have a stronger impact on reducing child measles mortality than immunization intensity of exposure is the major determinant of severe measles and measles vaccination should sharply reduce mortality. Vaccination programs have an indirect "herd immunity" effect: in a community where some are vaccinated children who are not will have less risk of intensive exposure to measles. Measles immunization policy in developing countries transmission patterns and control of measles and improved vaccination coverage are also detailed.

Title: Measles--United States, 1996, and the interruption of indigenous transmission.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: As of December 30, 1996 (week 52), local and state health departments had reported a provisional total of 488 confirmed cases of measles to CDC for 1996, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico had reported eight cases. In addition, indigenous transmission of measles in the United States was interrupted for a prolonged period beginning in late 1996. This report summarizes measles surveillance data for 1996, which indicate a substantial proportion of cases were associated with continued international importations of measles and outbreaks among school-aged children who were not required to receive a second dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV) to attend school.

Title: Live Measles Vaccine Not a Cause of Atypical Measles Syndrome
Author/s: Robert W. Amler; R. H. Rapkin
Abstract: In the August issue of PIR (3:55, 1981)1 there is an abstract regarding atypical measles which we believe is misleading and may deter vaccination of a group of adolescents for whom live measles vaccine is indicated. We have received calls from a number of concerned pediatricians and believe the abstract should be corrected. The author states that the atypical measles syndrome is a possible consequence of administering live measles vaccine to former recipients of killed measles vaccine. Although severe local reactions and occasionally systemic reactions do occur following administration of live measles vaccine to former recipients of killed measles vaccine, there is no evidence that the live vaccine causes classic atypical measles syndrome. Three patients, referred to as having severe systemic reactions by Krause et al,2 experienced fever to 103 F (39.4 C), malaise, marked local pain, swelling and erythema seven days following vaccination; these reactions lasted an average of eight days. However, the abstract was incorrect because these patients did not develop the atypical measles syndrome. Although the reactions were more severe than reactions that might be observed in persons who had never received killed measles vaccine, they were substantially less severe than the atypical measles syndrome. Atypical measles is characterized by high fever, pneumonitis (often with pulmonary consolidation and pleural effusion), pedal and hand edema, and an unusual rash which may be maculopapular, petechial, vesicular or urticarial. The rash is often most concentrated on the extremities.3 Revaccination with live measles vaccine is indicated for former recipients of killed measles vaccine to induce permanent immunity to measles, so that the atypical measles syndrome does not develop when the vaccinee is exposed to natural measles. Revaccination will not cause the syndrome. Atypical measles has occasionally been reported in individuals exposed to natural measles who had previously received live measles virus vaccine within a few months of receiving inactivated measles vaccine.4-6 This raises the possibility that the temporary immunity conferred by the killed vaccine precluded an adequate immunologic response to the live measles virus vaccine. Therefore, persons who received live measles vaccine within three months of receiving inactivated measles vaccine should also be revaccinated with live measles vaccine. Killed measles vaccine was used in the United States between 1963 and 1967 when an estimated 1,836,000 doses were distributed. Persons vaccinated with an unknown type of measles vaccine during that period should also be revaccinated with live measles vaccine as the unknown vaccine may have been killed measles vaccine.
Fields of Study: Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Rash; Malaise; Immunity; Pneumonitis; Measles vaccine; Attenuated vaccine; Medicine

Title: The Running Status of Measles Laboratory Network of Guizhou Province in 2008-2009
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To evaluate the running status of Guizhou provincial measles laboratory network.Methods To analyze surveillance results of measles laboratory network during 2008-2009.Results ①Serologic surveillance:4138 suspected measles cases were reported during 2008-2009,and 3814 measles sera samples were collected.The collection rate was 92.9%,and the rate of sample sending in time was 89.2%,and rate of laboratory diagnosis of measles cases was 86.2%.1278 sample measles IgM were positive and the positive rate was 33.5%,980 samples rubella IgM were positive and the positive rate was 25.7%.14 outbreaks were laboratory diagnosed as measles.②Virologic surveillance:7 strains of measles virus were successfully isolated from 42 throat swabs and urine samples in 2008-2009,all were certified as H1a genotype.③Laboratory quality control:Guizhou provincial measles laboratory passed the proficiency test and on-site review,and the provincial measles laboratory passed the sera samples recheck by National laboratory for measles during 2008 to 2009.qe municipal Center for Disease Control and Prevention(CDC)measles laboratory passed the sera proficiency test by the provincial measles laboratory(got full scores).The confirmation rate was 96.8% in the 472 sera samples sent by the municipal CDC measles laboratory.Conclusion The running status of Measles Laboratory Network of Guizhou province was good,which provided the technical support for suspected measles cases of the laboratory diagnosis and epidemiological surveillance.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Virology; Pediatrics; Measles; Measles virus; Rubella; Disease control; National laboratory; Proficiency test; Epidemiological surveillance; Medicine

Title: Measles Epidemiology in Ethiopia from 2006 - 2016: Predictors of High Measles Incidence from Surveillance Data Analysis
Author/s: Teklay K Desta; Ephrem T. Lemango; Jimma D Wayess; Balcha G Masresha Maternal; Fmoh Ethiopia; P.O. Box; Addis Ababa; Fmoh. P.O. Box; Regional Office for Africa; null Brazzaville; null Congo
Abstract: Background: Ethiopia endorsed the African Regional measles elimination goal and has been implementing the recommended strategies. Measles immunization coverage has been increasing but is still below the target, and measles incidence has remained high.; Objective: To describe the measles epidemiology in Ethiopia, identify predictors of high measles incidence in Ethiopia and recommend strategies to achieve the elimination goal.; Methods: Measles surveillance 2006-2016 data, routine immunization and post measles campaign coverage data was analyzed. We analysed the epidemiology and incidence of measles cases by age, vaccination status, year of occurrence, and geographic area.; Result: There were 66,719 confirmed cases, out of the 94,104 suspected measles cases reported between January 2006 and December 2016. Measles incidence increased from 20 cases per million total population in 2006 to 194 cases per million in 2015 and declined to 49 per million in 2016. On multiple logistic regression analysis, the median age of measles cases, the 2013 measles Supplemental Immunisation Activity (SIAs) coverage, the 2012 routine immunization coverage, and the proportion of reported under-five measles cases were predictors of very high measles incidence (>240 cases per million in the under-five years age population) in the three-year period following the 2013 measles SIAs implementation (p<0.01).; Conclusion: Ethiopia is not on track to achieve the measles elimination goal of less than 1 case per million population by 2020 with the current pace of elimination efforts. Accumulation of susceptible children due to suboptimal routine measles immunization combined with suboptimal and narrow age–group (9-59 months) measles SIAs resulted in continued measles outbreaks.; Recommendation: Ethiopia should scale up the quality and implementation of all the measles elimination strategies, including the introduction of measles second dose and conducting high quality measles SIAs targeting the appropriate age groups as per the measles epidemiology in various parts of the country to accelerate and achieve the 2020 measles elimination goal.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Outbreak; Measles; Population; Routine immunization; Total population; Surveillance data; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health
Keywords: Elimination; Epidemiology; Ethiopia; Incidence; Measles; Surveillance

Title: Measles immunization and its effectiveness in the southern Highlands.
Source Title: Papua and New Guinea medical journal
Publisher: Medical Society of Papua New Guinea
Abstract: Measles immunization was introduced in the Southern Highlands Province in 1982. Since then immunization coverage of children aged under 1 year has increased from 12% in 1982 to 52% in 1986. The number of reported measles cases has declined, despite an increase in the number of health facilities reporting and an increase in maternal child clinic visits where most sick children are seen. The incidence of measles and the incidence of diarrhoea are compared with the cumulative measles immunizations given. Problems with the Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI) campaign and means to improve measles vaccine coverage are discussed.

Title: Analysis on measles surveillance in Caoxian, Heze city, 2009-2013
Abstract: Objective To analyze the detection results of measles serum samples,the epidemic and clinical characteristics of measles cases so as to provide guidance for local prevention and control measles.Methods From the national disease surveillance information management system,the data of Caoxian measles epidemic,combined measles IgM and rubella IgM detection and isolation of measles virus of the suspected measles cases were conducted,and the epidemiological characteristics and classification of the cases were observed.Results A total of 227 cases of suspected cases of measles were reported,and serum sample collection rate of measles suspected cases was 72.69％ with the positive rate of measles IgM 18.71％,in 12 cases of throat swab specimens,5 strains of measles virus were isolated,genotype H1a confirmed.In all suspected measles cases,the people without measles vaccination reached 49.78％.168 cases were confirmed measles,and of which,the constituent ratios of the groups aged under ＜ 4 years were 80.95％.Preschool children and scattered children were the main group of measles,accounting for 89.29％.The main clinical manifestations included fever,more than 38.5℃ accounting for 86.63％,rash starting positions for the face accounting for 72.95％,with oral mucosal patches accounting for 72.80％.The top three complications of measles were pneumonia,myocarditis,Laryngitis.Conclusion Measles incidence was related with age,gender,occupation,season,mainly in sporadic.The measles virus was H1 genotype.; ; Key words: ; Measles ;  Immunologic surveillance
Fields of Study: Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; Disease surveillance; Measles virus; Rash; Rubella; Sample collection; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Analysis on measles and rubella laboratory network running situation in Beijing in 1999-2008
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Health Laboratory Technology
Abstract: Objective:In order to evaluate the work situation of measles and rubella laboratory network and discuss the function of laboratory surveillance in measles control.Methods: We analyzed surveillance results of measles and rubella laboratory during 1999-2008.Results: In total of 10506 samples collected from measles suspected cases,the positive rate of measles IgM was 47.73%,the positive rate of rubella IgM was 16.91%.All the measles and rubella outbreaks were confirmed by laboratory.In virologic surveillance,76 measles viruses and 32 rubella viruses were isolated.All the measles isolates were proved to be H1 genotype,except one D9 genotype.It means that H1 genotype is the dominated strains in Beijing.Conclusion: The Measles and Rubella Laboratory network is running well in Beijing,and it has played a very important role in measles control and elimination.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Virology; Measles; Beijing; Rubella; Medicine

Title: Effect of Measles Control in Guizhou Province,2003-2010
Abstract: Objective To analyze measles surveillance system from 2003-2010 and provide scientific basis for measles elimination in Guizhou Province.Methods Data of measles epidemic and laboratory surveillance was analyzed by the descriptive epidemiology.Results The incidence of measles in Guizhou Province reduced to 0.49/105 in 2005 with the help of strengthening mandatory immunization.Then the incidence rates rebounded in 2008 following the incidence rate of 3.11/105.After the follow-up SIAs,the incidence again was reduced to 0.22/105 in 2010.Since 2005,the proportion of rubella cases is increasing gradually in the reported suspect measles cases,.The provincial measles lab separated 5 strains H1a genotype wild measles virus in 2004 and 20 strains of the same virus from 2007-2010,which showed that the popular type of measles virus in Guizhou province had not changed.Conclusion The incidence of measles could be reduced to a lower level by strengthening mandatory routine immunization and by successfullt carrying out measles vaccine SIAs.
Fields of Study: Virology; Virus; Measles; Measles virus; Rubella; Measles vaccine; Routine immunization; Measles elimination; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Health ministry guidelines on measles immunisation.
Abstract: Measles is a highly infectious viral disease long known to mankind.  The virus is transmitted through air by respiratory droplets from infected children before a rash is seen.  In densely populated areas, measles is likely to occur year round, albeit with occasional seasonal peaks.  Providing the measles vaccine to children between 9 and 12 months of age may, however, significantly reduce levels of infant and child morbidity and mortality.  The paper describes how a measles case can be recognized, how measles is most severe in infants, the mystery surrounding the disease, the rationale for giving high priority to immunizing against measles, when and how the measles vaccine should be given, why measles immunization coverages are so low, why adequate immunization is often not done, and what health workers can do to improve coverage.

Title: Epidemiological Status and Related Factors of Measles in Changzhou City from 1984-2008
Abstract: Objective To analyze the epidemiological characteristics and related factors of measles in Changzhou City ,and to explore effective measures to improve the measles elimination strategy. Methods Measles epidemic data from 1984 to 2008 and measles surveillance data of the past five years were collected from routine report system for infectious disease and measles surveillance system ,and studied retrospectively. Results The measles incidence rate were below 3/100 000 from 1987 to 2004. New measles incidence peak emerged in 2005,2007,2008 respectively. The morbidity of males to females was 1.22:1 from 2 845 measles patients. The distribution of cases was between the 15-27th calendar weeks. The largest number of cases occurred in Wujin and Tianning district. Of all cases,1 917 patients reported as unvaccinated accounted for 67.38% and 1 640 patients of floating population accounted 57.64%. Conclusion The comprehensive measure including sensitive measles surveillance,high-quality management of measles and routine vaccine inoculation is an effective way to eliminate measles in Changzhou.

Title: Surveillance on measles in Caoxian County 2000～2002
Abstract: Objective To evaluate the present status of measles surveillance,improve the surveillance system and realize the measles control program. ELISA was adopted to survey the IgM-antibody in the blood of suspected measles and rubella patients. Epidemiological analysis was made on the data of legal report system of infectious diseases and measles report system. The measles report system had a better sensibility on epidemic situation report than the legal report system of infectious disease. The data of measles report system indicated that measles invasion had an obvious seasonal character,the invasion peak was from April to May;the invasion age was mainly under 15 years old. [Conclusion] Management on measles report system should be strengthened in order to improve the quality of routine and intensive measles immunization and keep measles into control.

Title: Epidemiologic characteristics of measles in 2006 in Hezheng County of Gansu Province and strategy for its prevention and control
Abstract: Objective To better understand the cause of high incidence of measles in Hezheng County, Gansu with description of its epidemiologic characteristics in 2006 and to explore strategies for its prevention and control.Methods Data of measles in 2006 in Hezheng County were collected from measles surveillance system and regular notification system of infectious diseases and analyzed by descriptive epidemiology.Results In 2006, totally 167 cases of measles were notified, with a reported measles incidence of 85.58 per 100?000 , 100～168.86 per 100?000 in some towns and townships, reaching the highest in the history of the County and significantly higher than that in the past years. Cases of measles focused in children aged under 15 years which accounted for 85.42 percent of the total cases, and during March to June. Young children aged more than eight months without history of immunization or with unknown history of immunization accounted for 71.54 percent of total measles cases. And, significantly less children of ethnic Hui and Dongxiang (4.76 percent) were immunized with measles vaccine than those of Han ethnic. Children in floating population constituted 9.8 percent of the total measles cases, 26.80 percent of them with history of measles immunization, 49.02 percent without it and 27.45 percent with history of immunization unknown.Conclusion Lower coverage of immunization was the main cause of higher incidence of measles in poor, remote and minority areas of Hezheng County. Implementation of strategies for measles control in poor and minority areas, including increasing governmental input for planned immunization work, strengthening management and improvement of cold-chain system of vaccine, especially improvement of conventional measles immunization, reinforced immunization for school-age children, and emergency immunization during measles outbreak, strengthening epidemiological and laboratory surveillance for measles, all are the keys to control and eradication of measles in poor, remote and minority areas.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Ethnic group; Immunology; Measles; Floating population; Measles vaccine; Immunization; High incidence; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Analysis of measles epidemiological characteristics of measles surveillance system in Huainan city in 2005
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Primary Medicine and Pharmacy
Abstract: Objective To the data of measles surveillance system(MSS)in Huainan city of Anhui province in 2005 in order to understand the measles epidemiological characteristics.Methods 421 suspected measles cases were reported.The cases distributed in 6 counties(districts).Both the sporadic and outbreak cases in the same districts ex- isted,19.24% cases were outbreak cases.In all measles cases,13.78%(58 cases)were below 8 months,62.22% (262 eases)were from 8 months to 15 years old,the rest accounted for 24.00%.The prevalent season was from March to June.In all measles cases,21.62% had measles vaccine hnmunization history.One dose 96.70%,the sec- ond dose were 3.30%,55.82% were non-vaccinated and 22.56% were uncertain.Results Measles cases were mainly due to low immunization coverage rate and failure of immunization coverage.Conclusion It is more impor- tant to carry out the measles“catch-up”immunization,the first immunization and revaccination must be strength- ened and vaccination quality must be improved.Effective strategy for measles control includes strengthening measles surveillances system,improving the immunization coverage rate and developing mass vaccination and so on.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Outbreak; Virology; Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Mass vaccination; Vaccination coverage; Medicine

Title: Analysis on Measles Epidemiology Status and Control Strategy in Guizhou Province in 2003～2005
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To Know the status of measles epidemic and accelerate the strategies for measles control.Methods The data of measles reported by the measles surveillance system in Guizhou Province in 2003～2005 were descriptively analyzed.Results The results showed that a total of 18943 measles cases were reported in the three years.We found that the distribution of the cases was sporadic and there was little change in age.The cases were children under 10 years old accounting for 83.5%.The peak seasons of the disease were in March-June.mainly in spring.191 were diagnosed as measles cases,the incidence of 0.49 cases per 100,000 population remained in 2005,it decreased 97.6% compared with in 2003,and 1/3 of the reported cases were confirmed as rubella.56 and 132 cases were diagnosed in one year old and under 7 years old,accounted for 29.3% and 69.1% of the total cases diagnosed,80.4%(45 cases)and 67.4%(89 cases)without measles vaccination records.Conclusion Measles incidence has been greatly decreased by measles mass campaign.We should strengthen revaccination for children of 18 months-2 years old and establish the regulation of immunization certificate for children going to kindergartens and primary schools,as well as the unvaccinated children.We intensify surveillance of measles,raise rate of diagnosed measles cases at laboratories,prevent rubella and continue measles mass campaign in 3 or 4 years.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; Rubella; Immunization; Population; Mass campaign; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Application of Spatial Regression Model for Modeling Measles Case in Indonesia
Author/s: Tuti Purwaningsih; Mutiara Herawati; Nanda Hadina Wijayanti
Abstract: Measles is also known as morbili in Latin and measles in English. Measles, in the past is considered as something that must be experienced by every child, they assume, that measles can heal itself if it was already out, so that children with measles do not need to be treated. This study examines the case of measles and the causes of measles. The variables used in the study were cases of measles (Y), population density (X1), immunization coverage (X2), average incidence (X3), and number of deaths (X4) in Indonesia covering all provinces. The study examined the pattern of spread, then given a SEM application to identify how much influence the measles factor can affect the case of measles in Indonesia. The results of the study show that Measles Cases in Indonesia have a regional grouping pattern. The modeling results using SEM show lambda and all significant variables. The SEM model produced AIC of 462,429 which was better than the regression of the SLM model with AIC of 467,499.
Fields of Study: Demography; Geography; Measles; Spatial regression model; Vaccination coverage; Incidence (epidemiology); Regression

Title: Implications of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus Epidemic for Control and Eradication of Measles
Source Title: Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America
Author/s: William J. Moss; F. Cutts; Diane E. Griffin
Abstract: Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)-infected persons may be important, unrecognized transmitters of measles virus, thwarting eradication efforts. We reviewed the published English-language literature on measles and measles immunization in HIV-infected persons to investigate the clinical features of measles, the responses to measles immunization, and the safety of measles vaccine in HIV-infected persons and, conversely, the effect of measles and measles immunization on HIV infection. HIV-infected persons with measles are likely to have uncharacteristic clinical findings and severe illness, with high rates of pneumonitis and death. Primary and secondary failure of measles vaccine in HIV-infected children may permit transmission of measles virus in spite of high rates of immunization coverage. A factor that complicates measles-control efforts in areas of high prevalence of HIV is the potential for fatal infection with measles vaccine virus. Further research on the impact of the HIV epidemic on measles and measles immunization is necessary to guide strategies for the eradication of measles.

Title: DYNAMIC ANALYSIS OF MEASLES EPIDEMIC DURING 1960-2005 IN XICHANG,SICHUAN
Abstract: [Objective]To explore and analyze long term trend of measles in Xichang,which will provide scientific evidences for measles control.[Methods]Data were from Xichang case report system for infectious diseases.Descriptive epidemiological analysis was used to explore the long term epidemic features of measles.[Results]There were 18 107 measles cases occurred during 1960 to 2005.The incidence rates showed a remarkable decrease trend during the 46 years.From 1960's to 1990's,the annual average incidence of measles per one hundred thousand dropped from 335.20 to 14.33,which was 95.72%decreased.The decrease was especially remarkable after measles vaccine adopted in this area.While the incidence dropped,the periodicity of measles epidemic was still existence.However,the peak of the periodical epidemics became lower and the peak interval became longer along with the time pass.[Conclusion]There is still a lot of work to do to reach the goal for controlling measles in Xichang.The most importance parts of the work are doing better in cold chain and increase the coverage of measles vaccine.

Title: A 24-year review on the epidemiology and control of measles in Singapore, 1981-2004.
Source Title: The Southeast Asian journal of tropical medicine and public health
Publisher: Southeast Asian Ministers of Education Organisation
Author/s: Gary Ong; Heng Bee Hoon; Adrian Ong; Lian Tee Chua; Chew Suok Kai; Goh Kee Tai
Abstract: The objective of our paper is to review the epidemiology of measles in Singapore and the impact of the measles vaccination program on the control of measles. Our review will form the basis for a critical appraisal of our future measles control program. We analyzed the trend of reported measles cases in relationship to measles vaccination coverage from 1981 to 2004 using routine measles notifications and measles vaccination data submitted to the Ministry of Health and the National Immunization Registry, respectively. We determined the measles vaccine efficacy using data from epidemiological investigations of reported institutional measles outbreaks. The herd immunity of the population against measles was accessed through three seroepidemiological surveys that we conducted in 1989/1990, 1993 and 1998. In addition, we collected blood specimens from every clinically diagnosed case of measles notified to the Ministry in 1998/1999 to for measles-specific IgM antibodies in order to evaluate the proportion of clinically diagnosed cases of measles that were laboratory confirmed. The incidence of measles has decreased significantly since 1981 as a result of increased vaccination coverage of 89-93% following implementation of compulsory measles vaccination in 1985. However, resurgences still occurred in 1992/1993 and 1997. With the implementation of the two-dose measles vaccination schedule, the annual number of laboratory confirmed cases of measles to date has been less than 150. Vaccine efficacy of the trivalent MMR vaccine based on institutional outbreak investigations was consistently above 92%. We also found that the overall seroprevalence of the population to measles has decreased from approximately 91.5% in 1989/1990 to 1993 to 77.9% in 1998 (mainly in children < 4 years old) and that only 7% of clinically notified cases of measles were serologically confirmed to be positive for measles. Achieving a vaccination coverage of more than 95%, tightening our MMR vaccine delivery system and strengthening surveillance of measles are essential components which must be addressed in order to interrupt measles transmission in Singapore.

Title: Development of Measles Elimination in China
Abstract: Although measles morbidity and mortality have been greatly reduced due to widely using measles vaccine(MV), the largest number of deaths of children are measles in the vaccine-preventable diseases. With the global eradication of smallpox and polio-free status in most areas of implementation, measles are on the WHO's list as the next infectious disease which intends to be eliminated. Measles elimination has become one of the main goals of WHO.WHO regional office for the Western Pacific commitment to eliminate measles in 2012. China's measles prevention and control work has made great achievements,but failed to achieve the goal of measles elimination in 2012.This paper reviewed the progress and explore the prospects of the measles elimination in China.

Title: Measles.
Abstract: <AbstractText Label="PURPOSE OF REVIEW" NlmCategory="OBJECTIVE">The purpose of this review is to summarize important papers concerning measles disease and measles-containing vaccines published in 2004.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="RECENT FINDINGS" NlmCategory="RESULTS">Endemic measles has been successfully controlled in the Americas and, to a lesser extent, in Europe. This has been achieved with a high uptake of two doses of a measles-containing vaccine. Even in industrialized countries, where vaccine uptake is poor, for example Japan, the disease is still a significant cause of morbidity and mortality. Vaccine failure is predominantly due to primary vaccine failure, which may, in part, be genetic in origin and related to HLA type. Measles-containing vaccines have been shown to be associated with febrile convulsions, but there is no strong evidence of a link with atopy. There is considerable evidence that there is no causal relationship with autistic disorders. In spite of this, many parents and some professionals have concerns about the safety of the vaccines, which may lead to their underuse.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="SUMMARY" NlmCategory="CONCLUSIONS">It is possible to eliminate measles with a high uptake of two doses of measles-containing vaccine, but concerns about safety persist and need to be tackled. More research is required into how to do this effectively and also to elucidate the causes of vaccine failure.</AbstractText>

Title: Analysis of the epidemiological characteristics of measles in Longyan city during 2005-2012
Abstract: Objective Measles epidemic characteristic analysis of longyan city in 2005-2012,to control measles epidemic and provide a scientific basis for formulate control measures.Methods Description epidemiological methods were used to analyse the epidemic data of measles,using SPSS17.0 statistical software.Results The annual incidence of measles in 2005-2008 was 2.58/100,000;and the incidence in 2009-2012 was 0.05/100,000.The incidence of measles was lower than 1/1 000 000 for four successive years,reaching the WHO's target of eliminating measles.Measles' cases were mainly concentrated from March to June.The cases in Xinluo,Zhangping,Yongding were more than other areas,in which students accounted for 58.98%,8~19 years olds accounted for 60.34%.The main influence factors were the local outbreaks of measles in primary and secondary schools.Conclusion The incidence of measles in Longyan was controlled below 1/1 000 000 since 2009,nearly reaching to the goal of eliminating measles.In order to reach the ultimate goal of eliminating measles,we should adhere to the strategy of high rate of immunization,improve the quality of measles' surveillance system and strengthen the ability of handling measles' epidemic emergencies.

Title: Progress toward global measles control and elimination, 1990-1996.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: In 1989, the World Health Assembly resolved to reduce measles morbidity by 90% and measles mortality by 95% by 1995, compared with disease burden during the prevaccine era. By 1996, the estimated incidence and death rates for measles worldwide were reduced by 78% and 88%, respectively. In 1990, the World Summit for Children adopted a goal of vaccinating 90% of children against measles by 2000. However, routine measles vaccination coverage has remained relatively stable since 1990, and an estimated 1 million children continue to die from this preventable disease each year. During the 1990s, the widespread use of innovative measles-control strategies in the Region of the Americas and countries such as Mongolia, South Africa, and the United Kingdom demonstrated that high-level measles control and even interruption of transmission is feasible over large geographic areas. This report updates the status of measles control and elimination worldwide and includes disease surveillance and vaccination coverage data received by the World Health Organization (WHO) headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, as of August 29, 1997. These findings indicate that, in some regions, substantial progress has been made to control and interrupt measles transmission; in others, measles continues to cause high morbidity and mortality because of failure to implement measles-control strategies.

Title: Achieving measles control: lessons from the 2002–06 measles control strategy for Uganda
Author/s: William B. Mbabazi; Miriam Nanyunja; Issa Makumbi; Fiona Braka; Frederick N. Baliraine; Annet Kisakye; Josephine Bwogi; Possy Mugyenyi; Eva Kabwongera; Rosamund F. Lewis
Abstract: BACKGROUND The 2002-06 measles control strategy for Uganda was implemented to strengthen routine immunization, undertake large-scale catch-up and follow-up vaccination campaigns, and to initiate nationwide case-based, laboratory-backed measles surveillance. This study examines the impact of this strategy on the epidemiology of measles in Uganda, and the lessons learnt. METHODS Number of measles cases and routine measles vaccination coverage reported by each district were obtained from the National Health Management Information System reports of 1997 to 2007. The immunization coverage by district in a given year was calculated by dividing the number of children immunized by the projected population in the same age category. Annual measles incidence for each year was derived by dividing the number of cases in a year by the mid-year projected population. Commercial measles IgM enzyme-linked immunoassay kits were used to confirm measles cases. RESULTS Routine measles immunization coverage increased from 64% in 1997 to 90% in 2004, then stabilized around 87%. The 2003 national measles catch-up and 2006 follow-up campaigns reached 100% of children targeted with a measles supplemental dose. Over 80% coverage was also achieved with other child survival interventions. Case-based measles surveillance was rolled out nationwide to provide continuous epidemiological monitoring of measles occurrence. Following a 93% decline in measles incidence and no measles deaths, epidemic resurgence of measles occurred 3 years after a measles campaign targeting a wide age group, but no indigenous measles virus (D(10)) was isolated. Recurrence was delayed in regions where children were offered an early second opportunity for measles vaccination. CONCLUSION The integrated routine and campaign approach to providing a second opportunity for measles vaccination is effective in interrupting indigenous measles transmission and can be used to deliver other child survival interventions. Measles control can be sustained and the inter-epidemic interval lengthened by offering an early second opportunity for measles vaccination through other health delivery strategies.

Title: Analysis of an adult measles outbreak in the eastern part of Ehime prefecture in Japan
Source Title: [Nihon koshu eisei zasshi] Japanese journal of public health
Publisher: Nihon Eisei Gammai/Japanese Society of Public Health
Abstract: PURPOSE An outbreak of adult measles occurring in the eastern part of Ehime prefecture in Japan was analyzed, and the future directions of measures against infectious diseases in public health centers was studied. METHOD An outbreak of adult measles (in individuals more than 18 years old) occurred in the eastern part of Ehime prefecture between October 2002 and July 2003. During this outbreak, provisional surveillance of adult measles as well as pediatric measles (less than 18 years old) was performed by all clinics and hospitals in addition to that by The Ehime Epidemiological Surveillance of Infectious Diseases (sentinel surveillance). Furthermore, identification of viral genes was performed from pediatric and adult measles patient samples. RESULTS 112 cases of measles in adults and 200 cases in children were reported in Ehime between October 2002 and July 2003, and the rates for the eastern part of the prefecture accounted for 89.7% and 94.1% respectively. The relation between the pediatric measles and adult measles could be followed by change in the outbreak number of patients week by week. The genotype of the measles viruses isolated was type H1, which is prevalent in China and Korea. The results of an epidemiological survey showed that 59.3% of adult and 84.1% of pediatric cases had not been vaccinated. The measles vaccination rates for adult and pediatric cases were 21.9% and 11.4%, respectively. While the number of measles cases detected by sentinel pediatric surveillance comprised 32.0% of the measles cases reported by all pediatricians, the figure for adult measles cases was only 11.6% of those reported by all clinics and hospitals. CONCLUSIONS It was suggested that improvement in the vaccination rates and revaccination rates mainly among school children or young people is necessary to prevent measles outbreaks. The surveillance system must be strengthened to increase the number of sentinel hospitals because an outbreak may not be detected with the current approach, especially for measles among adults. Positive information exchange between public health centers and medical institutions also appear to be particularly important.

Title: Effects of measles-control activities--African region, 1999-2005.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: In 1999, of approximately 871,000 deaths from measles worldwide, 61% occurred in sub-Saharan Africa. In 2001, countries in the World Health Organization (WHO) African Region began an accelerated measles-control program to reduce by half by 2005 the number of deaths that were caused by measles in 1999. The African Region accelerated measles-control program was based on four strategies: improving routine vaccinations; providing a second opportunity for measles vaccination through a routine, 2-dose vaccination schedule or through supplementary immunization activities (SIAs); improving measles case management; and establishing case-based surveillance with laboratory confirmation for all suspected measles cases. Seven countries in the region had already completed catch-up SIAs by 2000, before the regional program began; in 2001, additional countries in the region began implementing catch-up, and later, follow-up SIAs, and steps were taken to improve routine vaccination coverage with measles vaccine and other vaccines in the Expanded Programme on Immunization schedule. This report summarizes the nationwide SIAs and other measles-control activities conducted in the WHO African Region during 1999-2004, analyzes the trends in reported measles cases since 1990, and compares the annual number of measles cases reported in 2005 with those reported in 1999.

Abstract: Objective To evaluate the effectiveness of measles Supplementary Immunization Activities(SIAs) of measles attenuated live vaccine(MV),explore the effective measures for controlling measles,and provide a scientific basis for measles elimination.Methods Descriptive epidemiology method was used to analyze the data from 2009 to 2010 according to the questionnaires for measles cases,measles surveillance system and the national disease surveillance information reporting system,etc.Results The reported coverage rate and sample investigation coverage rate were 98.66% and 99.21% of the target children,respectively.The coverage rate was over 97% in each age group.There were 118 suspected measles cases reported from measles surveillance system,and 110 measles cases reported from the national disease surveillance information reporting system in 2009.Accordingly,the number of cases was 14 and 7 in 2010,respectively.The measles incidence rate decreased by 93.73% in 2010 after SIAs conducted in 2009.The positive rate of measles IgM antibody was 80% in 2009 and 28.57% in 2010.22.22% of the cases aged 8 months or younger and 16.24% aged above 15 years.There were 62.71% of children who had never received any dose of measles vaccination at all and 3.64% had been injected twice or more.Conclusion The measles SIAs of MV carried out in 2009 played an important role in measles elimination.However,measles immunization strategy should be further studied to help eliminate measles.

Title: Measles pneumonitis following measles-mumps-rubella vaccination of a patient with HIV infection, 1993.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: The Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) recommends measles-mumps-rubella vaccine (MMR) for all persons asymptomatically infected with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and recommends that MMR be considered for all symptomatic HIV-infected persons who would otherwise be eligible for measles vaccine, because measles virus infection can cause severe illness and death in such persons. Serious or unusual adverse events in HIV-infected persons after receiving MMR have not been reported previously. This report summarizes the investigation of a case of progressive vaccine-associated measles pneumonitis in a person with acquired immunodeficiency syndrome and provides interim recommendations for the use of measles-containing vaccine among HIV-infected persons.

Title: Analysis on the operation of measles monitoring system in Jiangxi Province in 2004
Abstract: Objective The study was designed to further understand the epidemic feature of measles and the operation of measles monitoring system in Jiangxi Province. Methods An analysis was made on the conventional report system for communicable diseases of Jiangxi Province in 2004 and the suspected cases of measles reported through the measles monitoring and report system as well. Results There were 3300 cases of measles reported from measles monitoring system in 2004, which were 4.92% less than from the conventional report system for communicable diseases. No dead case was reported, and the incidence rate was 7.72/ Lakh. The measles cases were broadly distributed with epidemic features of sporadic and outbreak existing at the same time, and local measles outbreak influenced the measles incidence strength of the whole province. The cases were mainly in 92 counties (districts) of 10 cities of the whole province, and the susceptible seasons mainly from March to June, in which the incidence cases was 53.82% of the total, and the incidence mainly among preschoolers and pupils, which were 77.61% of the total cases. Among the cases, there were 60.30% without history of immunity or history record of immunity, of which 66.91% were children aged from 8 months to 15 years. Conclusion The routine measles immunization and re-inoculation work of Jiangxi province should be strengthened, and exerting efforts to improve the monitoring quality of measles monitoring system will be the main task of Jiangxi province for accelerating measles control.

Title: THE RELEVANCE OF MEASLES TODAY
Source Title: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)
Abstract: Urgench branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Urgench, Uzbekistan✓ ResumeThis article outlines the main issues related to the etiology, epidemiology and pathogenesis of measles infection, taking into account the data of the last outbreak of this disease in 2023-2024. The classification of measles, the features of its clinical course in children in the age aspect are presented, the characteristics of the clinical forms of the disease with central nervous system damage are given, and the complications of measles are described. The features of the formation of immunity in measles are shown. The issues related to the laboratory and differential diagnosis of this infection are outlined, modern approaches to the treatment and specific prevention of measles are presented in detail, and the need for vaccination within the framework of the National Calendar of Preventive Vaccinations is emphasized. The requirements for antiepidemic measures in the measles outbreak and dispensary monitoring of children who have had measles infection are considered.Keywords: complication, vaccination, contagiousness, age, rash, common, prevention.Introduction. Measles is a highly contagious acute viral disease that is transmitted by airborne droplets and can lead to serious complications and death. Between 2000 and 2021, measles vaccination prevented 56 million deaths. Despite the availability of a safe and cost effective vaccine, an estimated 128,000 measles deaths were reported worldwide in 2021, mostly among unvaccinated or under-vaccinated children under the age of 5. In 2022 About 83% of children in the world received one dose of measles vaccine during the first year of life as part of planned public health care, which is the lowest rate since 2008. Prior to the introduction of the measles vaccine in 1963 and the widespread use of measles vaccination, major epidemics were recorded approximately every two to three years and led to approximately 2.6 million deaths annually.Measles is an acute infectious viral disease with a very high level of contagiousness, the causative agent of which is the measles virus. It is characterized by high fever (up to 40.5 C), inflammation of the mucous membranes of the oral cavity and upper respiratory tract, conjunctivitis and a characteristic spotted papular rash of the skin, general intoxication. According to the World Health Organization, in 2018, about 140 thousand people died from measles worldwide, most of them children under the age of five. Measles remains one of the prominent causes of child mortality in developing countries. The number of measles cases in the world increased dramatically in 2017. Due to gaps in vaccination coverage, measles outbreaks have been observed in all regions.Etiology. The causative agent of measles is an RNA virus of the morbillivirus genus of the paramyxovirus family, which has a spherical shape. The virus consists of a nucleocapsid — minus-an RNA strand, three proteins and an outer shell formed by a matrix protein and two surface glycoproteins: one of them is hemagglutinin, the other is a "fusion" protein. The virus remains active in the air and on surfaces for up to 2 hours, it is poorly resistant in the external environment, quickly dies outside the human body from the effects of various chemical and physical factors (irradiation, boiling, treatment with disinfectants). There is a hypothesis about the origin of the measles virus from the bovine plague virus during the emergence of animal husbandry. Despite the instability to the external environment, there are cases of the virus spreading over considerable distances with the flow of air through the ventilation system — in the cold season in one single building. Weakened strains of the measles virus are used to produce a live measles vaccine.Epidemiology. The success of immunization made it possible to classify measles as "outgoing" infections included in the National Measles Elimination Program (Order No. 192 of the Ministry of Health of the Republic of Uzbekistan dated April 24, 2003), the purpose of which was to eliminate this disease in our country by 2020. The program included several stages: at the first stage, it was planned to achieve widespread stabilization of measles incidence rates at a sporadic level in all regions of the country, at the second — to create conditions for preventing the occurrence of measles cases and complete eradication of measles infection in the Republic of Uzbekistan, at the third (2018-2020) — certification of measles-free territories. However, in 2019-2023, the situation changed dramatically: the incidence of measles in the Republic of Uzbekistan increased. The first cases of the disease were imported from countries such as the United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, France, Finland, Russia, India, Malaysia, and China. Title: Progress in global measles control and mortality reduction, 2000-2006.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: The World Health Organization (WHO) and United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) comprehensive strategy for measles mortality reduction is focused on 47 priority countries. Components include 1) achieving and maintaining high coverage (>90%) with the first dose of measles vaccine by age 12 months in every district of each priority country through routine immunization services; 2) ensuring that all children receive a second opportunity for measles vaccination; 3) maintaining effective case-based surveillance and monitoring of vaccination coverage; and 4) providing appropriate clinical management, including vitamin A supplementation. In 2005, the World Health Assembly set a goal for global measles control as part of the Global Immunization Vision and Strategy (GIVS): a 90% reduction in measles mortality by 2010, compared with 2000 levels. In January 2007, WHO/UNICEF reported that implementation of measles mortality reduction strategies had reduced measles mortality by 60%, from an estimated 873,000 deaths in 1999 to 345,000 deaths in 2005. This reduction exceeded the goal of 50% measles mortality reduction by 2005 (compared with 1999 levels) that had been set in 2002. This report updates previous reports by detailing 1) measles mortality reduction activities implemented during 2006 and 2) the impact of activities since 2000 on the global burden of measles and progress toward the GIVS mortality reduction goal for 2010.

Title: Measles in Norway. Epidemiology before and after the introduction of routine vaccination
Source Title: Tidsskrift for den Norske laegeforening : tidsskrift for praktisk medicin, ny raekke
Abstract: Twenty years after routine vaccination against measles was introduced in Norway its incidence has been reduced by over 97%. Measles encephalitis has not been reported in Norway since 1982 and the last registered death from the disease was in 1985. There is evidence of a certain increase in the age of measles patients since the introduction of vaccination but this has not led to any increase in the death-to-case ratio. The vaccines (measles vaccine from 1969 and MMR from 1983) have proved safe in use, with few reported probable or possible reactions. The vaccination programme has resulted in a large reduction in measles morbidity but if the goal of eradicating endemic measles in Norway by 1990 is to be achieved, then coverage must be further improved.

Title: Measles control--can measles virus inhibitors make a difference?
Source Title: Current opinion in investigational drugs (London, England : 2000)
Author/s: Richard K. Plemper; James P. Snyder
Abstract: Infection by measles virus (MV) is a major cause of human morbidity and mortality worldwide. In 2001, the WHO, UNICEF and their partners launched the Measles Initiative, the goals of which are to interrupt the transmission of MV in large geographic areas by increasing vaccination coverage and to assess the feasibility of eradicating MV worldwide. An estimated 74% reduction in mortality resulting from measles was achieved between 2000 and 2007, equivalent to a reduction of approximately 200,000 deaths annually. Despite this progress in the control of measles, the highest number of measles cases in more than a decade was observed in 2008 in several European countries and the US, and the virus was again declared endemic in the UK. In the light of this resurgence in the UK and the limitations associated with the current live-attenuated vaccine, this review discusses the means by which safe and effective measles antivirals could augment vaccination and strengthen global efforts to control measles. Important aspects of treatment are the potential to prevent infection effectively after exposure to MV, the improvement of case management, the amelioration of complications that frequently follow MV infection and the influence of antivirals on a potential strategy for global measles eradication.

Title: Monitoring on Immune Successful Rate of Measles Vaccine and Measles-Mumps-Rubella Vaccine in Jiangsu
Abstract: Objective To know the immunogenicity of measles vaccine and measles-mumps-rubella vaccine after expanded program on immunization,and to provide a basis for elimination of measles and control of mumps and rubella. Methods The sera samples of 120 8-month-old infants and 117 children aged from 18 to 24 months were collected before and 4 weeks after immunization of measles vaccine and measles-mumps-rubella vaccine,respectively.Then the antibodies were determined. Results The circulating measles antibody acquired from mother in 8-month-old infants disappeared.The positive rate of antibody to measles after immunization of measles vaccine was 100%.The positive rate of antibody to measles in 117 children aged from 18 to 24 months was 99.15%,but it was 100% after revaccination with MMR vaccine.The average content of antibody to measles in the 117 children was 5,077.11 mIU/ml,but it was 14,410.57 mIU/ml after revaccination with MMR vaccine.The positive rates of antibodies to mumps and rubella were 93.91% and 96.46%,respectively. Conclusions The immunogenicity of measles vaccine is good.The current measles vaccine,the program and the dose are feasible and effective.Revaccination with MMR vaccine is significant in remarkably increasing the antibody to measles and effectively protecting children from measles,mumps and rubella.
Fields of Study: Virology; Vaccination; Measles; MMR vaccine; Rubella; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Immunogenicity; Measles-Mumps-Rubella Vaccine; Medicine

Title: Analysis of epidemiological characteristics of measles in Suizhou,2006-2008
Abstract: Objective To analyze epidemiological characteristics of measles in Suizhou,2006-2008,and to provide a scientific basis for strategy development of measles elimination.Methods A descriptive epidemiological analysis was performed for measles cases in the city.Results The incidence rate of measles was 10/100 000 in year of 2006-2008,during which epidemic situation was observed in May and June.Students of elementary and middle schools accounted for 50.77% of cases,while chidren of kindergartens and others for 30.77%.There had been increasing trend of incidence rates of measles in children under 4 years old and in teenage of 12-16 years old.Conclusion It is necessary to increase the coverage of measles vaccination for children of 2-7 years old and to vaccinate infants of eight month old in timely fashion.Improvement of the vaccination inspection system and strengthen of comprehensive measures including measles surveillance in critical areas and population are important for measles control and prevention.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Vaccination; Measles; Population; Strategy development; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Study on Immunoprotecive Efficacy of S191 Measles Vaccine to the Epidemic Strains in Shanxi
Abstract: [Objective]To Analyze the immunity of measles vaccines in Shanxi province to learn immunoprotecive efficacy of current measles vaccines.[Methods]Neutralization antibody level of measles vaccine virus and for wild measles virus in sera from healthy children and acute measles patients were determined by trace neutralization test.[Results]NT antibody geometric mean titer(GMT) in the serum of vaccines and naturally infected individuals with the history of immunization to anti-wild type measles virus was 61.04 and 30.24 respectively,which was obviously lower than the GMT to S191(GMT was 105.61 and 83.72 respectively).The antibody negative rate against wild strain was 16.7% to these sera with the history of measles vaccination,and antibody potency against S191 strain less than 8.[Conclusion]Antigenic variation of wild-type measles virus that circulated in Shanxi presented obviously and immunoprotecive efficacy of measles vaccine was partly influenced.
Fields of Study: Neutralization; Virology; Immunology; Vaccination; Virus; Measles; Measles virus; Immunity; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Medicine

Title: Study on genotyping and vaccination-related factors of measles virus in Shanxi province
Abstract: Objective The pathogenic strain to result in measles epidemic in Shanxi province was identified,to search for factors concerning measles vaccination.Methods Samples of throat swabs from measles patients were collected for isolation of measles virus;to obtain RNA from measles virus suspensions by the phenol-chloroform extraction method,to amplify 450 nucleotide segments(bp) at carbon-terminal of N-gene by reverse transcription polymerase-linked reaction(RT-PCR),and to identify the sequence and genotype of the amplified segments.The neutralization(NT) antibody levels for measles vaccine virus and for wild measles virus in sera from healthy people and acute measles patients were determined by trace neutralization test.Results A total of 82 samples of throat swabs were collected,with 19 strains of positive measles virus isolated,these strains were H1 genotype of measles virus.The anti-wild type measles virus NT antibody geometric mean titer(GMT) in the serum of vaccines and naturally infected individuals with the history of immunization were 26.36 and 26.90 respectively).which was obviously lower than anti-measles vaccine strain(GMT was 49.30 and 56.18 respectively).Conclusion Measles epidemics in Shanxi province recent years were resulted by H1 genotype measles virus,possibly partly because measles vaccine cannot induce adequate protective level to current epidemic strain.
Fields of Study: Genotype; Neutralization; Virology; Vaccination; Virus; Measles; Measles virus; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Biology; Microbiology

Title: HUBUNGAN STATUS IMUNISASI DAN KETEPATAN IMUNISASI CAMPAK DENGAN KEJADIAN PENYAKIT CAMPAK DI KABUPATEN BANYUMAS
Abstract: Background: Approximately 30,000 children of Indonesia die annually due to measles complications. Campak outbreaks occurred at District of Banyumas in 2008 with Attack Rate (AR=115), Case Fatality Rate (CFR=16.67%) and in 2009 outbreaks occurred three times. Achievement of Universal Child Immunization (UCI) of villages at District of Banyumas in 2009 was 99.4%. Program of measles immunization should reduce incidence rate of measles disease.; Objective: To identify correlation between immunization status and time appropriateness in measles immunization and the incidence of measles disease at District of Banyumas.; Method: The study was analytic observational with case control method. Cases were underfives diagnosed as having measles disease at health center and included in the surveillance program of Banyumas District Health Office in 2007-2011. Control comprised healthy underfives registered in the same integrated service post with the cases. Samples of each case and control consisted of 113 people. The independent variables were immunization status and time appropriateness in immunization whereas the dependent variable was the incidence of measles disease. Data analysis used bivariate technique.; Result: There were as may as 113 cases of measles disease in 2007-2011. The result of bivariate analysis showed score of measles immunization was OR=2.733, 95%CI 1.329-5.783 and p=0.008 and time appropriateness in measles immunization was OR=3.085, 95%CI 1.793-5.307 and p=0.00, both were associated with the incidence of measles disease at District of Banyumas.; Conclusion: Underfives that did not get measles immunization on time appropriateness had risk for having measles three times greater than those that had it on time appropriateness at District of Banyumas.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Pediatrics; Case fatality rate; Immunology; Measles; Immunization; Measles disease; Measles complications; Attack rate; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Epidemiological Analysis and Control Strategies of Measles in Guangdong Province
Source Title: Guangdong Journal of Health and Epidemic Prevention
Abstract: Objective To determine the epidemiological characteristic of measles in Guangdong Province in different periods and to develop a series of strategies for measles control. Methods Analyzing the morbidity of measles, the immunization coverage rate of measles vaccine (MV) and the measles antibody level in population from 1951 to 2000.Results: The morbidity of measles in pre-vaccine period (639.10/100 000, 1951～1966), vaccine application period (436.40/100 000, 1967～1978), EPI period (163.10/100 000, 1979～1986) and EPI-cold chain period (4.35/100 000, 1987～2000) showed a significant decline trend and measles incidence peak a delay trend also. Most measles cases were under 10-year old, accounting for 78.8%. A total number of 172 227 500 person-doses MV were used from 1967 to 2000. The MV coverage rates were in the range from 84 8% to 95.9% in 1979～2000. The immunization successful rates and seropositive rates were in the range from 88.9% to 94.4% and from 87.4% to 98.1% respectively in 1994～2000.Conclusion:1. Measles was effectively controlled in Guangdong Province. 2. Strategies should be taken in measles control and elimination, including: (1) Maintenance high routine vaccination coverage ("Keep-up") and high vaccination success rate; (2) Implementation of measles "Catch up" and "Follow up" complementary approaches; (3) Establishment and improvement of the measles surveillance system.

Title: Progress toward measles elimination--Japan, 1999-2008.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: In 2005, the Regional Committee of the World Health Organization (WHO) Western Pacific Region (WPR) set a target date of 2012 for measles elimination in all WPR member states. In Japan, measles control strategies have included 1) a nationwide public awareness campaign implemented in 2001 to promote timely vaccination with the first dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV1) administered on or after age 12 months, and 2) a 2-dose MCV schedule with the second dose (MCV2) administered at age 5-6 years, adopted in 2006 in accordance with the recommended WPR measles elimination strategy. However, during 2007-2008, Japan experienced a large measles outbreak, which resulted in exportation of measles cases from Japan into countries where measles elimination had been achieved. This report describes the epidemiology of measles in Japan during 1999--2008 and approval of a National Measles Elimination Plan in December 2007 that includes recommendations for immunization strategies, case-based measles surveillance, and monitoring to ensure elimination of measles by 2012. Measles continues to be endemic in Japan, with most cases occurring in children before school entry, except for 2007 and 2008, when a shift to an older age group was observed. With implementation of the National Measles Elimination Plan, Japan is expected to make progress toward achieving the WPR measles elimination goal.

Title: EPIDEMIOLOGICAL CHARACTERISTIC ANALYSIS OF MEASLES EPIDEMIC SITUATION IN LUOYANG IN 2006
Abstract: [Objective]Through analyzing measles epidemiological characteristics in Luoyang in 2006, to provide scientific evidence for prevention strategy and measures for measles. [Methods] Descriptive epidemiological method was used to analyze the data on measles epidemic situation report and monitoring systemic in Luoyang in 2006. [Results] The incidence rate of measles was 14.1 / 100 000, and was highest level in 10 years. In local population, highest measles incidence rate happened during 0-4 years old, accounted for 63.4% in total incidence (600 / 946) ; measles primarily happened between March and June, and that prompted the successful immunization ratio did not reach the request. Late spring and early summer are the key time to prevent and control measles. [Conclusion] Strengthening measles vaccination for 0-4 year-old children, paying attention to the vaccine quality, reinforcement immunity and initiative monitoring on measles situation, finishing emergency vaccination work of susceptible children and close contacts, will be the next key prevention and control work for measles.
