Title: Measles Immunity among New Health Personnel at a Faculty of Medicine in Khon Kaen Province, Thailand, 2019
Abstract: Measles continues to be an important global health problem despite the implementation of vaccination. Health personnel, a high-risk group, are considered to have immunity to measles. We aimed to investigate the measles immunity status among health personnel during pre-placement at the Faculty of Medicine, Khon Kaen University, northeastern Thailand. The sample included health personnel who were examined in the pre-placement program from October 2018 to September 2019. Data collected included sex, age, department, job characteristic (direct or indirect contact with patients), position, measles immunity status, based on serology tests, history of previous infection, and history of immunization. A total of 652 health personnel were included, of which 53.8% tested positive to measles IgG class antibodies, indicating prior exposure to the virus. Among them, the percentage testing positive to measles immunity was 59.5%. Two-thirds of personnel who could not recall their infection or vaccination history had a positive measles immunity status. A consensus for the definition of acceptable presumptive evidence of measles immunity in Thailand according to context must be stipulated, leading to improved management of measles immunization. Title: A measles outbreak among people with measles vaccination history in Beijing
Abstract: A measles outbreak occurred in Beijing in 2013. 5 cases were diagnosed,they all received more than 2doses of measles vaccine 8- 12 years ago. The serological detection indicated that measles IgM were negative in all cases and measles IgG titers in convalescent phase were 4 times higher than that in acute phase in 2 cases. Nucleic acid testing for swab specimens confirmed that the pathogen was measles virus genotype H1. Secondary measles immunization failure might be the main cause of vaccinated people to be affected in this outbreak. Virus nucleic acid detection plays an important role in the diagnosis of measles due to secondary immunization failure.

Title: Progress toward elimination of measles from the Americas.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: In 1994, the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) established the goal of eliminating measles from the Western Hemisphere by 2000. To reach this goal, PAHO developed a measles-elimination strategy that includes three vaccination components ("catch-up," "keep-up," and "follow-up") and integrated epidemiologic and laboratory surveillance. The aim of the strategy is to achieve and maintain high levels of measles immunity among infants and children and detect all chains of transmission of measles virus through careful surveillance. This report updates measles surveillance data through February 1998 and summarizes the impact of elimination strategies on measles in the Americas.

Title: EPIDEMIOLOGICAL STUDY OF MEASLES IN BIHAR

Abstract: CONTEXT: Measles is one of the leading causes of death in young children despite the availability of safe and effective vaccine. There is need to fasten the immunization project in Bihar in order to decrease the measles outbreak. AIM: The aim of this study was to study the epidemiology of measles in Bihar during September 2011 to December 2012. SETTING AND DESIGNS: One year three month study witch is based on data of measles surveillance project of Bihar. RESULT: This includes the total number of measles case and measles outbreak in Bihar during Sep 2011 to Dec 2012. The total number of measles case were 165(52.7%) and total number of measles outbreak were 47. the children that were immunized but developed disease were 22(32.9%) and the children that were unimmunized and developed disease were 143(57.2%). eight death were recorded. CONCLUSION: High vaccination coverage with two doses of measles and rubella containing vaccine should be introduced. This will decrease the outbreak and mortality.

Title: Clinical characteristics of 207 measles cases with etiological diagnosis
Abstract: Objectives To understand the influence of measles vaccination on clinical manifestations of measles in children, different characteristics of measles in infants and children in order to improve early diagnosis of measles by analyzing the characteristics of measles cases with laboratory confirmed etiological data. Methods Clinical data were collected from children who visited the Department of Outpatient Infectious Diseases and hospitalized with clinical diagnosis of measles and confirmed by measles specific IgM and measles virus N gene fragment from January 2002 to December 2010. Measles specific IgM antibodies were tested using serum samples with ELISA. Measles virus N gene fragment was amplified from specimens of throat swabs and fresh urine by reverse transcription-polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR). Analyses were performed in vaccinated and unvaccinated cased separately. Results ① In total, 207 cases of measles in children with etiological diagnosis were analyzed, including 123 males and 84 females, 53 vaccinated and 154 unvaccinated. Age distribution ranged from 0 to 15 years, including 69 cases (33.3%) of younger than 8 months (who had not reached the age for primary measles vaccination), and 45 cases (21.7%) of younger than 1 year of age. ②Cases with Beijing local residency was more common in vaccinated group than that in unvaccinated group. The peak incidence was found in April for all cases in two groups. 15.4% (24/154) cases with known exposure history in measles unvaccinated group and in unvaccinated group and 16.9% (9/53) in measles vaccinated group, with no significant difference between two groups. ③The proportions of measles with symptoms of mucosa spots ( Koplik spots) and cough were significantly lower in vaccinated group than in unvaccinated group while the other symptoms had no significant difference between two groups. ④ Of 154 unvaccinated cases, 97 were younger than 1 and 57 older than 1 year old. Symptoms of eyes was more common in the subgroup older than 1 year of age. Conclusions Measles cases with younger age have been increasing in recent years in Beijing. More and more measles cases for children have presented with atypical clinical manifestations. Most of the measles cases in this study were younger than 8 months of age which is before EPI for measles vaccination or older than 8 months of age but missed vaccination. The data indicated that strengthening measles immunization program was necessary, and early and rapid etiological diagnosis for atypical measles was important for early isolation of the cases.

Abstract: Objective To analyze the laboratory test results for measles in huainan from 2004 to 2011,and provide a diagnostic evidence for measles control and elimination.Methods Serological test and descriptive epidemiology method were used in suspected measles cases from measles surveillance system.Results A total of 851 serum samples of suspected measles cases were detected from 2004 to 2011,with 457(53.7%) were measles IgM-positive cases.Suspected measles cases with positive IgM results covered all the counties.The peak incidence was found during the period of March to May.Most cases aged below 8 months and above 15 years.Conclusion Early serological diagnosis of suspected measles by detection of IgM antibodies could provide basis for determination of measles epidemic and taking measures timely.

Title: Measles (Rubeola): An Update.
Source Title: Journal of special operations medicine : a peer reviewed journal for SOF medical professionals
Author/s: Elena M Crecelius; Mark W Burnett
Abstract: Measles is a significant concern with approximately 10 million people infected annually causing over 100,000 deaths worldwide. In the US before use of the measles vaccine, there were estimated to be 3 to 4 million people infected with measles annually, causing 400 to 500 deaths. Complications of measles include otitis media, diarrhea, pneumonia, and acute encephalitis. Measles is a leading cause of blindness in the developing world, especially in those who are vitamin A deficient. Malnourished children with measles are also at higher risk of developing noma (or cancrum oris), a rapidly progressive gangrenous infection of the mouth and face. Most deaths due to measles are caused by pneumonia, diarrhea, or neurological complications in young children, severely malnourished or immunocompromised individuals, and pregnant women. A rare sequela of measles is subacute sclerosing panencephalitis.

Title: Analysis on surveillance of measles in Rizhao city in 2005—2011
Abstract: Objective To provide the basis of strategies and measures for eliminating measles by analyzing the epidemiological characteristics of measles from 2005 to 2011 in Rizhao.Methods The epidemiological data of measles from 2005 to 2011 was analyzed using descriptive epidemiology.Results 193 measles cases were reported from 2005 to 2010,there is no case in 2011.The average annual reported measles incidence was 0.97/100 000.Measles cases distribute in all counties,but there is difference between counties.The measles peaked at March to June.The age of onset is mainly above 15 yeas old.Sporadic and outbreak were all concomitant and the main cases were local in sporadic.There were three outbreaks and the cases were all imported.Conclusion Significant progress on measles elimination was achieved in Rizhao in future,the high level based immunization rates among children should be continued to maintain and consolidate.The MV SIA should be complete with high quality,strengthen measles surveillance and risk assessment.Do the early outbreak warning and control work for eliminating measles.

Title: Progress toward global measles control and regional elimination, 1990-1997.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: In 1989, the World Health Assembly resolved to reduce measles morbidity and mortality by 90% and 95%, respectively, by 1995, compared with disease burden during the prevaccine era. In 1990, the World Summit for Children adopted a goal of vaccinating 90% of children against measles by 2000. Regional measles-elimination goals have been established in the American Region (AMR) by 2000, the European Region (EUR) by 2007, and the Eastern Mediterranean Region (EMR) by 2010. This report updates progress toward global measles control and regional elimination, and presents measles vaccination coverage and incidence for 1997 and WHO estimates of global measles morbidity and mortality in 1997 compared with the prevaccine era.

Title: Measles: An Ongoing Threat.
Abstract: Measles is an acute, serious, highly contagious viral infection. Despite the existence of an effective and safe vaccine for more than half a century, measles outbreaks continue in the United States and cases are increasing worldwide. Further, there is a decreasing trend in national measles, mumps, and rubella vaccination coverage among kindergarteners. This article is intended to provide practitioners caring for children with an overview of the history of measles, its current national and international epidemiology, clinical picture, and important complications, as well as methods for prevention. Readers are directed to in-depth reviews for specific detailed recommendations regarding vaccines and biologics mentioned herein. [Pediatr Ann. 2025;54(5):e167-e173.].

Title: Analysis of Effect and Cost-benefit on Measles Vaccination in Shenzhen
Abstract: Objective To analyze the effect and economic benefit of measles vaccination,and to provide scientific basis for eliminating measles in 2012.Methods The data of measles vaccination and immunization monitoring from 1980 to 2004 in Shenzhen were analyzed,and cost-benefit analysis method was used to analyze the economic and social benefits of measles vaccination.Results The results of measles surveillance in 2004 showed that the antibody positive rate was 83.6% in population.Investment cost of measles vaccination from 1983 to 2004 was $55,088,159,product benefit was $395,538,712,net benefit was $340,450,553,and ratio of cost and benefit was 7.18 in Shenzhen.Social benefit analysis showed that 195,376 individuals avoided suffering from measles from 1983 to 2004.Conclusion Measles vaccination can bring enormous economic and social benefit.
Fields of Study: Cost–benefit analysis; Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Cost benefit; Social benefits; Benefit analysis; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Data Analysis of Measles Monitoring in Yangquan City in 2006
Abstract: [Objective]To analyze the epidemiology of measles in Yangquan,provide scientific basis for measles control and eradication.[Methods]Data of measles from 2006 in Yangquan were epidemiologically analyzed.[Results]261 suspected measles cases were reported in 2006 in Yangquan,the incidence rate was 19.97100 000.There was a difference of the incidence in each counties(P0.001).The epidemic peak of measles was from March to May.The highest incidence was among children under 14,accounting for 62.45%.Preschool children accounted for 72.41%.The ratio of male to female was 1.21:1.28.35% of children had immune history.71.65% had no immune history or unknown immune history.The positive rate of measles serum IgM was 59.23%.[Conclusion]The EPI in our city is still weak;the coverage of routine immunization was lower.The main causes of measles epidemic were that the outbreak of measles can not be timely reported and handled.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Outbreak; Pediatrics; Immunology; Measles; Routine immunization; Male to female; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Detection of Measles Virus,Rubella Virus,and Mumps Virus by MRT-PCR
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To discover a rapid MRT method for diagnosis of measles,rubella,and mumps infection. Methods MRT-PCR method was developed for detection of measles virus,rubella virus,and mumps virus. Results The measles vaccine strain(Shanghai-191),rubella vaccine strain(BRD Ⅱ),mumps vaccine strain(S_(79)),the live attenuated measles-rubella-mumps vaccines and the clinical samples collected from the measles,rubella,and mumps patients were analyzed.The amplified fragments of measles virus,rubella virus,and mumps virus in one reaction were 635bp,411bp,and 519bp respectively. Conclusion The sensitivity of this method to detect measles virus,rubella virus,and mumps virus was 1TCID_(50),1TCID_(50),and 1TCID_(50) respectively.We developed a specific,sensitive,and rapid MRT-PCR method for diagnosis of measles,rubella,and mumps infection.
Fields of Study: Viral culture; Virology; Mumps vaccine; Rubella virus; Measles virus; Mumps virus; Rubella; Rubella vaccine; Measles vaccine; Medicine

Title: Analysis of measles surveillance data, Sokoto State, Nigeria: 2010 - 2016
Source Title: Pan African Medical Journal Conference Proceedings
Author/s: Ismail Abdullateef Raji; Muhammad Shakir Balogun
Abstract: Introduction: measles is a disease that has a worldwide distribution; however, control efforts have substantially altered the global distribution. The incidence has decreased substantially in regions where vaccination has been instituted. In spite of the increase in Measles immunization coverage aimed at building population immunity against the disease, Measles outbreaks still occur, especially in the developing countries like Nigeria. The aim of this study was to describe the distribution of Measles cases in Sokoto State in time place and person. Methods: descriptive analyses of Measles Surveillance data between 2010 and 2016 was carried out to describe the epidemiology of Measles in Sokoto State in time, place and person. Microsoft excel and Epi info were used to analyze the data. Proportions, cross tabulations and chi square test were used to find associations between variables. Results: a total of 13974 suspected cases were line listed between January 2010 and June 2016, there were a total of 13974 suspected Measles cases. Overall, 11107 (80%) were below 5 years and 6553 (47%) were females. Majority, 12896 (92%) of the suspected Measles cases were reported in the first half of each year of evaluation. Gwadabawa Local Government Area (LGA) recorded the highest number of Measles cases 1881 (14%). Ten thousand and twenty-seven (72%) of suspected Measles cases did not receive Measles vaccine. The overall case fatality rate (CFR) was 0.9%, however, Bodinga LGA had the highest CFR of 4% and over the period of surveillance year 2015 had the highest CFR (1.8%). Only 605(4%) suspected Measles cases were tested for Measles IgM. One hundred and twenty-five (0.9%) deaths were recorded; factors significantly associated with deaths are number of doses of Measles received (p = 0.001) and admission status (p < 0.001). Conclusion: there is persistence of Measles over the period affecting primarily children below five years. This may be due to vaccinations being missed by children despite activities to strengthen coverage. We recommend that there should be more awareness programs and strengthening of Measles immunization in the State.
Fields of Study: Herd immunity; Epidemiology; Outbreak; Pediatrics; Case fatality rate; Vaccination; Measles; Measles vaccine; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Measles & malnutrition.
Source Title: The Indian journal of medical research
Publisher: Medknow Publications and Media Pvt. Ltd
Abstract: Measles is an important acute childhood viral infection having severe consequences on the nutritional status. The adverse nutritional effects of measles are experienced by both the well-nourished and the malnourished children. However, the severe nutritional deficiencies like kwashiorkor/marasmus are precipitated only in children who are already malnourished. As high as 3-4 per cent of children with measles suffered from these clinical nutritional syndromes in their post-measles period. Though malnutrition is widespread among Asian children also, measles appears to run a milder course with low mortality rates in developing Asian countries, as compared to African children. The associated secondary infections which apparently complicate the primary illness in malnourished children might be responsible for higher mortality and could be due to socioeconomic and environmental causes that are associated with poverty and malnutrition rather than due to malnutrition or measles per se. Measles related blindness is of multifactorial aetiology. While acute measles triggers corneal ulceration through viral proliferation in the cornea, nutritional keratomalacia is often the cause of blindness in the post-measles period. Measles vaccination is the major preventive measure. However, timely use of local antibiotic therapy to the eyes and administration of vitamin A supplements offer protection to the child who already has measles. Response of malnourished children to live attenuated measles vaccine has been found to be safe and effective. Neither malnutrition nor tuberculosis which are widespread among malnourished children of developing countries appear to be contraindications for measles vaccination. Thus, the beneficial effects of the measles vaccination should be fully exploited by adequate supply of potent vaccine and coverage of all susceptible children.

Title: Analysis on measles epidemiological characteristics and strategies of measles elimination from 2007 to 2013 in Huangshan City
Abstract: Objective To analyze the epidemiological characteristics and summarize selated wocks towards measles elimination in Huangshan city from 2007 to 2013,so as to provide evidence for measles elimination. Methods The surveillance data and elimination strategy of measles were analyzed by descriptive epidemiological method. Results The average annual incidence of measles was 1. 37 /100 000 population from 2007 to 2013 in Huangshan city,with the highest rate being 2. 72 /100 000 population in 2008. By measles-containing vaccine( MCV) supplementary immunization activities( SIA) in 2009,the incidence decreased by 77.14% comparing with that in 2010. By MCV SIA again in 2010 and 2011,the lowest rate was 0. 47 /100 000 population in 2012. The peak of measles incidence still concentrated March to May.Greater incidence of measles was concentrated in ≥ 15 years population,accounting for 64. 14% of the total cases. Conclusion Implementation of MCV SIA could quickly reduce the incidence of measles to a lower level. But in order to achieve the eventual elimination of measles elimination and consolidate the achievements,we must keep routine immunization coverage rate high,take measles control strategies and carry out related adult research as soon as possible.

Title: Measles vaccination: why and how?
Author/s: H C Rümke; E J Ruitenberg
Abstract: The measles virus is highly contagious and may hit non-immune populations very hard, as observed on remote islands. The first live-attenuated measles virus vaccine was registered in the United States in 1963, and was imported to the Netherlands from 1968 onwards. Production was taken over by the National Institute for Public Health (RIV). Because the burden of disease was still high, measles vaccination was introduced into the Dutch National Immunisation Programme in 1976; since 1987 this has been in the form of the combined measles, mumps and rubella (MMR) vaccination. The MMR vaccine was also initially imported and later manufactured by the National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM). Since then, measles epidemics have almost exclusively affected unvaccinated populations. Vaccinated individuals are thus well-protected, as are unvaccinated individuals as long as the rate of vaccination in the surrounding population is sufficiently high. Unvaccinated individuals who travel to countries where measles is endemic are still at a higher risk. Recent studies show that measles not only has the classical symptoms, but also damages the immune system.

Title: Measles Mass Campaign Experience and Effect Evaluation on Measles Vaccine in Guizhou Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To analyze the results of measles mass campaign in Guizhou Province and discuss future strategies for measles control in Guizhou.Methods Comprehensively analyzing the data of the immunization coverage rate of measles mass campaign,the surveillance of immune level and Measles Surveillance System.Results Total 8,611,528 children were collected,both the report coverage rate and the fast assessment coverage rate were over 95%,after mass campaign,public measles IgG antibody positive rate and the GMT level increased significantly.Measles incidence rate decreased over 98% after measles mass campaign.The cases of less than 5 years old and less than 1 year old in 2006 accounted for 80.8%,37.0% of the total cases,the cases of the group increase,about 81.9% with no or unknown immunization history,and about 1/3 were confirmed as rubella.Conclusion With successful implementation of measles mass campaign in Guizhou Province,high quality measles mass campaign is the important strategy on prevent and control rapidly measles outbreak and epidemic.In order to remain the achievements of measles mass campaign,to raise routine immunization coverage rate,strengthen the regulation of immunization certificate for children going to kindergartens and primary school,and continue measles mass campaign after three or four years.
Fields of Study: Immunology; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Immunization history; Routine immunization; Measles outbreak; Vaccination coverage; Mass campaign; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Investigation on Measles Supplementary Immunization in Bobai County in 2006
Abstract: [Objective]To analyze the effect of measles supplementary immunization in Bobai county in 2006,and find out a way to control measles.[Methods]Measles vaccine supplementary immunization campaign was conducted for the target children aged from 8 months to 10 years in Bobai county in 2006.Data from on-the-spot investigation,assessment report and from notifiable communicable diseases reporting system were analyzed.Comprehensively analysis of.[Results]Totally 235 782 targeted children were imunizaed,the reported coverage was 95.20% and the evaluation coverage was 95.71%.The positive rate of measles IgG antibody after supplementary immunization campaign was 97.54%,and the protective rate was 90.16%.[Conclusion]Measles supplementary immunization was effective in Bobai county in 2006,maintain the high rate of routine measles immunization,and implementing measles supplementary immunization and follow-up mass campaign,were the effective way to control measles.

Title: Analysis on the Results of Serum Detection in Suspected Measles Cases,Jinan City,2008-2010
Abstract: [Objective]To analyze the results of serum detection and the incidence of measles in Jinan city,2008-2010,so as to provide the data support for eliminating the measles.[Methods]Enzyme linked immunosorbent assay(ELISA) was used in the serum detection of measles and rubella.IgM antibody was detected for the measles suspected cases from the administer area in Jinan.[Results]There were 2 152 suspected cases in three years.1 345 were measles antibody positive,the positive rate was 62.50%(P0.01);171 were rubella antibody positive.The positive rate was 7.95%(P0.05).The higher incidence was in 2008.The groups of less than 2 years and between 20 to 39 years had a higher measles incidence.[Conclusion]The measles incidence is declining year by year,The groups of less than 2 years and between 20 to 39 years become the main groups in measles epidemic.

Title: Evaluation on Effect of the Supplementary Immunization of Measles Vaccine in Baoshan City,2008 and 2010
Abstract: [Objective]To evaluate the effect of the supplementary immunization of measles vaccine in Baoshan city,2008 and 2010.[Methods]Descriptive analysis was made according to vaccination rate of measles vaccine for supplementary immunization,data from the field investigation and the legal measles epidemic reporting system and sample survey of antibody level.[Results]The two-round vaccination rate of measles vaccine was all above 95%.At the same time,the antibody level of measles greatly improved to 95.02% and the incidence of measles greatly decreased from 5.98/105 to 1.08/105 of 2009 and 0.12/105 of 2010,finally there were no measles cases which have been reported so far.[Conclusion]The incidence of measles is effectively controlled after the two-round supplementary immunization of measles vaccine.

Title: Measles Vaccination Coverage and Anti-Measles Herd Immunity Levels in the World and WHO Regions Worsened from 2019 to 2023.
Abstract: <AbstractText Label="OBJECTIVES" NlmCategory="OBJECTIVE">The objectives of this study were as follows: to determine mean percentages of measles vaccination coverage with zero, one and two doses of vaccine and anti-measles herd immunity levels in World Health Organization (WHO) regions in 2023; to assess variations in measles vaccination coverage and anti-measles herd immunity-related indicators from 2019 to 2023; and to assess whether zero-dose measles vaccination coverage indicators were on track to achieve the Immunization Agenda 2030 objective.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="METHODS" NlmCategory="METHODS">Mean percentages of vaccination coverage with two, one and zero doses of measles vaccine in WHO regions in 2023 were calculated using data from the WHO/UNICEF global and regional immunization information system.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="RESULTS" NlmCategory="RESULTS">In 2023, the global mean two-dose measles vaccination coverage was 65.3%, and mean two-dose vaccination coverage was lower than 95% in all WHO regions; the mean prevalence of measles-protected individuals in the target vaccination population was 87.6%, and anti-measles herd immunity levels in the target vaccination population were sufficient to block the transmission of measles viruses with greater transmissibility (R<sub>o</sub> ≥ 15) only in the Western Pacific and European WHO regions. The global mean two-dose measles vaccination coverage decreased by 3.7% from 2019 to 2023. In 2023, the mean zero-dose measles coverage and number of zero-dose measles children were, respectively, 36.7% and 40.6% greater than the values required to be on track to achieve the 2030 objective.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="CONCLUSION" NlmCategory="CONCLUSIONS">This study found that all measles-vaccination-coverage-related indicators worsened from 2019 to 2023, and the zero-dose measles vaccination coverage and number of zero-dose measles children in 2023 were not on track to achieve the AI2030 objective. Interventions to increase routine two-dose measles vaccination coverage should be developed in all WHO regions.</AbstractText>

Keywords: MCV1; MCV2; WHO regions; anti-measles herd immunity; measles elimination and eradication; measles prevention strategies; measles vaccination; measles zero-dose coverage; two-dose measles coverage

Title: Geographical and time trends of measles incidence and measles vaccination coverage and their correlation in Nigeria.
Author/s: Ryoko Sato; Olusesan Ayodeji Makinde; Koeloengan Clement Daam; Bola Lawal
Abstract: Nigeria has one of the highest measles burdens in the world. While measles-containing vaccine is proven to be effective in reducing measles cases, empirical studies on the correlation between measles incidence and measles vaccine coverage in Nigeria has been limited. The aim of this study was to conduct a detailed analysis on measles incidence, measles vaccine coverage, and their correlation between 2012 and 2021. A retrospective observational study was conducted based on the Integrated Disease Surveillance and Response (IDSR) data for the measles incidence in each Nigerian state over time, District Health Information System, V.2 (DHIS2) and Nigeria Demographic and Health Survey (DHS) for the coverage of the first dose of measles containing vaccine (MCV1) over time (2012-2021). We observed the trend of measles incidence and measles vaccine coverage, as well as their correlation. Out of the study period from 2012 to 2021, we found that the majority of measles outbreaks occurred in the northeastern states in recent years after 2019, especially in Borno state, where Boko Haram insurgency has negatively impacted health service delivery, including routine vaccination. We observed a significant negative correlation between measles incidence and measles vaccine coverage across Nigerian states. However, there was no sudden drop in measles vaccine coverage before the recent outbreak in Borno state in 2019, which could be due to various factors other than the overall vaccine coverage.

Title: Epidemiological Characteristics of Measles in Rizhao City during 2005-2010
Abstract: [Objective]To analyze the epidemiological characteristics and preventive measures of measles in Rizhao City during 2005-2010,and provide evidence for developing strategy and measures to eliminate measles. [Methods]The descriptive epidemiology was applied to analyze the incidence of measles in Rizhao City during 2005-2010 and the adoption of strategy and measures to eliminate measles. [Results]The reported measles incidence was 0.071/100 000 in 2010 which achived the goal of eliminating measles preliminarily.The peak incidence of measles appeared during March to June from 2005 to 2010,children aged ≥ 15 years had the highest incidence.Most measles cases had not immune history or unknown immune history of MV.Three measles outbreaks occurred;measles cases of 2 outbreaks were all imported cases. [Conclusion]Significant progress on measles elimination has been achieved in Rizhao City.High quality SIA and leakage searching with vaccination are the important means to reduce the incidence of measles and block the spread of measles virus.In future,the high level based immune immunization rates among children should be maintained and consolidated continuously.Suspected measles cases surveillance,early warning and control of outbreaks should be conducted so as to achieve the goals of eliminating measles thoroughly.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Outbreak; Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Immunization; Descriptive epidemiology; Measles elimination; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Experience with the use of pharmacopoeial reference material for the activity of measles vaccine
Author/s: A S Binyatova; T N Yunasova; R A Volkova; A Y Butirskiy; T N Ilyasova; K А Sarkisyan; A A Movsesyants
Abstract: <AbstractText Label="INTRODUCTION">Due to the increased incidence of measles in Russia and in many other regions of the world, vaccines for the measles prevention are especially in demand. Ensuring the quality of the measles vaccine for effective disease prevention is within the scope of the tasks of the state policy of our country.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="OBJECTIVE">Evaluation of the experience of using a pharmacopoeial standard material of measles vaccine activity for measurement of the specific activity of the measles virus in vaccines with a measles component that are used in the Russian Federation for measles prevention.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="MATERIALS AND METHODS">The object of the study was the Pharmacopoeia reference material (PRM) of the activity of the live measles vaccine of series 10. The activity of PRM was analyzed when determining the specific activity of the measles, mumps-measles and combined vaccines for the prevention of measles, rubella and mumps, as well as based on the materials of the summary protocols for the production of these vaccines.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="RESULTS">The titer of the measles virus in the PRM for each determination of the specific activity of the measles virus in vaccines in the Scientific Centre for Expert Evaluation of Medicinal Products in 2021-2022, as well as according to the summary production protocols, was within the boundaries of the certified value (4.63 ± 0.5) lgTCD<sub>50</sub>/0.5 ml, and the test results met the acceptance criteria in accordance with the requirements of regulatory documentation. During the observation period, the average value of the PRM titer (4.61 lgTCD<sub>50</sub>/0.5 ml) practically did not differ compared to the average value of the certified characteristics of the PRM, the standard deviation of the mean value of the measles virus titer in the PRM did not exceed 0.15 lgTCD<sub>50</sub>, which indicated the stability of the analytical work at the enterprise and in IC.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="CONCLUSION">The data obtained indicate the stable activity of PRM, the correctness of the determination of the measles virus titer in the vaccination dose of the vaccine, and the validity of the method for monitoring the specific activity of the measles virus in vaccines.</AbstractText>

Title: Measles surveillance in Kashmir: A mixed methods study.
Author/s: Khalid Bashir; Khurshid Ahmad Wani; Mariya Amin Qurieshi; S Muhammad Salim Khan; Inaamul Haq
Abstract: <AbstractText Label="Background" NlmCategory="UNASSIGNED">Measles surveillance serves as the means of monitoring program success. The quintessential purpose of measles surveillance is to identify gaps and garner effective public health responses to achieve measles elimination.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="Objectives" NlmCategory="UNASSIGNED">There were two key objectives: (i) to conduct an in-depth review of the existing measles surveillance system in Kashmir and highlight its strengths and weaknesses and (ii) to assess the pattern of measles-containing vaccine (MCV1) coverage and MCV2 coverage among under-5 years children and describe the health-seeking patterns of suspected cases of measles.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="Methods" NlmCategory="UNASSIGNED">The mixed methods study was conducted in the Kashmir valley from March 2018 to March 2019. An explorative qualitative design was followed using individual face-to-face interviews with thirty-two (n = 32) different stakeholders from the state, district, medical block, and primary health center (PHC) levels. To complement the qualitative study, a quantitative survey was done in two districts, Srinagar and Ganderbal, which consist of 5 and 4 medical blocks, respectively.</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="Results" NlmCategory="UNASSIGNED">Among the suspected cases of measles, 52% had visited PHCs. Sixty-four suspected cases of measles (64) were immunized with two doses of MCV. None of the clinically suspected cases of measles were further investigated. In the qualitative analysis, five themes were generated viz, "measles surveillance description of Kashmir valley;" "factors affecting measles surveillance, perceptions, and experiences of stakeholders;" "barriers to measles surveillance;" "measles surveillance activities need to be intensified;" and "respondent recommendations for building an effective and sensitive measles surveillance system."</AbstractText>;           <AbstractText Label="Conclusion" NlmCategory="UNASSIGNED">The current measles surveillance system in Kashmir was not effectively functioning; case-based measles surveillance is not being done as per the WHO guidelines. There is a lack of planning, advocacy, awareness, and communication of measles surveillance among the stakeholders. The visible barriers in measles surveillance included lack of training, logistics, incentives, and monitoring by internal and external agencies.</AbstractText>

Title: Assessment on Measles Vaccine Strengthening Immunization in Measles Accelerating Control in Hebei Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective Evaluating the effect of measles accelerating control strategy in Hebei Province,in which the measles vaccine strengthening Immunization was implemented. Methods We analyzed the data of measles vaccine routine immunization,measles vaccine "catch-up" and "follow-up" immunization campaign and data of measles cases of epidemic and laboratory surveillance during 1997～2005. Results According to Hebei Province's measles accelerating control strategy,we insisted to carry out measles vaccine routine immunization and keep the high coverage rate.We have implemented measles "catch-up" and "follow-up" immunization campaign during 1998～2001 and 2002～2005,respectively.The measles cases incidence rate decreased greatly in the whole Province since then.The sensitive measles surveillance system has been established and the measles cases diagnosis in serology and pathogen level,for which to support the strategy and the implementing of the measles control have been improved.Meanwhile,by analyzing the problems in measles accelerating control work,proposed measles elimination strategy suggestion in the year of 2012 in Hebei Province. Conclusion Great progress had made in measles accelerating control work in Hebei Province,and the success in measles vaccine immunization and measles cases surveillance strategy have established the measles elimination.
Fields of Study: Virology; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Routine immunization; Measles elimination; High coverage; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Investigation on Measles Prevalence in Wucheng District,Jinhua City,2008
Abstract: Objective To investigate the epidemiological characteristics of measles prevalence in Wucheng District,Jinhua City,in 2008,and to provide scientific strategies for measles control.Methods Measles incidence and immunization history of measles patients in Wucheng District were analyzed by case study.Results A total of 298 suspecfed measles cases were reported in Wucheng District in 2008,among which,26 were clinical diagnosed,101 were laboratory diagnosed,and 171 were excluded.Compared with 42 cases in 2007,the reported cases increased by 202.38% in 2008.The ratio of male to female was 1∶0.81 in measles cases.Among the patients,80.32% were without immunization history or with uncertain immunization history of measles vaccine.Conclusion Persons without immunization history were high risk population.Strategies including strengthening of measles routine immunization and timely supplementary immunization should be focused on.

Title: Measles Antibody Level in the 136 Mothers of Measles Cases under 8 Months in Henan Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To know the measles antibody level of measles cases mother,and provide evidence for establishing scientific strategies for measles elimination.Methods 136 measles cases mothers were chosen for case group,and 272 non measles mothers were control groups,blood samples were collected and measles immunoglobulin(Ig)G antibody were detected.Results the positive rate of the case group is 98.53%(134/136),geometric mean titer is 1:8016.78,and the IgG antibody level of the case group is higher than control groups(97.79%).Conclusion Because of inapparent infection or mother and the infant infected measles virus simultaneously or successively,the antibody level of the case mother group is higher than the control groups.It is one of strategies for measles control among infants to conduct health education to the reproductive women.

Title: Study on Clinical Characteristics of 371 Measles Children Arising With Healthcare Proposal in Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Obstetrics and Gynecology
Abstract: Objective; To review the clinic properties of measles during epidemic outbreak in Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture, and discuss further its implications for prevention.; ; ; Methods; A retrospective analysis was made on measles children. The regional vaccine innoculations, clinical symptoms, laboratory examination and therapeutic results from an epidemiological view were summarized from 371 cases from July 2006 to June 2007. Measles diagnosis was relied on clinical history and symptoms: measles exposure, 2~4 days' prodrome, together with fever, respiratory catarrh, light intolerance, lacrimation and Koplik spots in oral mucosa. In addition, the typical measles rash sequence is the sufficient diagnostic proof that rashes start from face and head, spread to the lower body, persist for 3~4 days, and fade with pigmentation.; ; ; Results; The peak period of measles was between October and March. More than 80% cases had the typical rash history, and patients whose ages were lower than 3 years and 8 months accounted for 76. 5% and 22. 6% respectively, suggesting a high propensity of measles occurrence at lower age. 62. 5% of vaccinated patients did not immune to measles,which suggesting vaccination efficiency should be ameliorated. A typical measles diagnosis was supported by the finding of measles-specific IgM in the serum without the attenuated live measles vaccine inoculation in one month.A statistical significance was tested by Chi-square.; ; ; Conclusion; The average age of susceptible measles population begins to decrease in children, especially those under 3 years. Improved the measles inoculation program would be promising to prevent the measles outbreak.; ; ; Key words: ; measles; measles vaccine; susceptible population; infants

Title: Evaluation of Effect after Emergency Vaccination of Attenuated Living Measles Vaccine
Abstract: Objective Evaluated the effect after Emergency Vaccination of Attenuated Living Measles Vaccine in Xiamen City.Methods Carried out Attenuated Living Measles Vaccine to 1 to 14 years old children,including floating children and residential children without measles vaccination.Results After Attenuated Living Measles vaccinating,the measles incidence was decreased from 12.09/100000 to 8.50/100000,with the extent of 29.69%.The measles incidence in children from 1 to 14 years was decreased from 47.11/100000 to 22.21/100000,with the extent of 52.86%.But the low coverage of measles vaccine of floating children influnced the incidence decline of the group of 1 to 4-year-old.Conclusion The measles incidence was decreased significantly with carrying out catch-up immunization campaign.Epidemic situation was controlled effectively and anticipated goal was achieved.

Title: Can measles be eradicated globally
Source Title: Bulletin of the World Health Organization
Abstract: Measles is one of the most infectious diseases. Before measles vaccine was introduced, nearly everyone contracted the disease at some point in childhood. By the late 1980s, most countries had incorporated measles vaccine into their routine immunization programmes. Globally, about 800 000 children nevertheless still die from measles annually, half of them in Africa. Eradicating measles would therefore play an important role in improving children's survival. The 24th Pan American Sanitary Conference in 1994 established a goal of eradicating measles from the Americas. Progress to date has been remarkable and the disease is no longer endemic in the Americas, with most countries having documented interruption of transmission. As of November 2003, 12 months had elapsed since the last indigenous case was detected in Venezuela. This experience shows that measles transmission can be interrupted, and that this can be sustained over a long period of time. Global eradication is feasible if an appropriate strategy is implemented. Even under a new paradigm in which immunization is not discontinued after measles is eradicated, eradication will be a good investment to avoid expensive epidemics and save the lives of almost one million children annually. A world free of measles by 2015 is not a dream.

Title: Monitoring report of measles antibody in Luliang county
Abstract: OBJECTIVE To understand IgG antibodies of measles in healthy population in Luliang county. METHODS The application of enzyme linked immunosorbent assay(ELISA)was carried out to detect measles IgG antibody in serum of 759 healthy human. RESULTS The positive rate of measles IgG antibody in serum was 99.08%. In different counties(towns)and different genders,there were no significant differences. The positive rates of measles IgG antibody were not different with and without measles vaccine(MV)vaccination history. The positive rate of measles IgG antibody had no correlation with MV inoculum. CONCLUSION In Luliang county,it has established a certain immunity barrier of measles in healthy population.

Title: Analysis on Epidemiological Characteristics of Measles before and after Emergent Immunization of Measles Attenuated Live Vaccine in Jilin Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective In order to know measles epidemiological characteristics in Jilin province before and after measles mass campaign,and to provide evidence for measles elimination.Methods Analyzing information of National Notifiable Diseases Reporting System and casebased information of Measles Surveillance System by descriptive epidemiology.Results Measles incidence decreased 86.5% after emergent immunization within 2 years than the average measles incidence.Measles incidence(8.7/100,000)increased in the third year after emergent immunization,but still decreased 67.5% compared with the average measles incidence before emergent immunization.The most proportions of measles cases were 0-14 years old.The measles virus circulated in 2009 were relative small branch compared with those circulated from 2005 to 2008.All the measles virus in 2009 showed 3 common amino acid changes in 150 amino acids of C-terminal of N protein.Conclusions Emergent immunization of measles vaccine decreased Measles epidemic quickly.In the fact,however,the most measles cases are the aged 0-4 group that remind us to enhance routine immunization.The following mass campaign should be conducted according to the measles epidemiological characteristics,laboratory surveillance data and vaccination rate.

Title: Analysis on Risk Factors of Measles Among Children Under the Age of 8 Months in Baoan District,Shenzhen
Abstract: Objective To explore the risk factors of high incidence of measles among children aged below 8 months in Baoan District,and then take the corresponding measures for measles control.Methods A case-control study was carried out among the children aged below 8 months for seeking risk factors,such as hospital exposure history,history of contact with measles cases,mother’s history of measles,mother’s history of vaccination,and population mobility so as to determine the causes of prevalence.Results Nosocomial exposure and contact with measles cases were the risk factors which made this disease spread,and the OR values were 4.2 and 3.5,respectively.Mother’s history of measles and mother’s history of vaccination were the protective factors,and the OR values were 0.1 and 0.2,respectively.Strata analysis showed that history of contact with measles cases,hospital exposure history,and nosocomial exposure to measles virus were the risk factors,and the 95% confidence interval was 0.87～23.51,1.27～20.70,and 1.52～83.66,respectively.Conclusions Nosocomial infection and history of contact with measles cases are the risk factors of high incidence of measles among children aged below 8 months in Baoan District,while mother’s history of measles and mother’s history of vaccination are the protective factors.The results suggest that the time of measles vaccination should be ahead of schedule.

Title: Measles Epidemiological Characteristics and Progress of Measles Elimination in China, 2010
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective This study is to analyze the epidemiological characteristics and summarize progress towards measles elimination in China in 2010. Methods The national measles surveillance data were analysed by descriptive epidemiology. Results The reported measles incidence was 2.86 per 100,000 population in 2010(27.59% lower than that in 2009). The number of measles cases reported from Hebei, Heilongjiang, Beijing, Henan, Tianjin took account for 72.00% of the total numbers. The highest measles incidence occurred among children aged 5 years old, followed by people aged 25-29 years, 30-34 years and 5-9 years. Non-measles cases reported was 1.39 per 100 thousand population, proportion of suspected measles cases investigated within 48 hours of report was 88.87%. Proportion of suspected cases with adequate serum specimens was 76.63%. Percentage of serum specimen results reported within 7 days of collection was 84.54%. Of the 316 Measles outbreaks, 94.94% were laboratoryconfirmed and 40.19% with specimen collected for virus detection. Conclusion China achieved significant progress on Measles elimination in 2010. For the next step, China need to further strengthen the routine immunization program, to conduct high-quality measles attenuated live vaccine supplementary immunization activities and catch-up vaccination in some priority regions. It is necessary to strengthen measles surveillance system and response outbreak for interrupting transmission of measles virus.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Virology; Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; Transmission (medicine); Measles virus; Immunization; Population; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: MOTHERS AND HEALTH PROMOTION: CLOSING THE GAP FOR 100% MEASLES IMMUNIZATION IN NORTH VIETNAM
Source Title: Malaysian Journal of Paediatrics and Child Health
Author/s: Huong Thi Thu Nguyen; Marguerite C. Sendall
Abstract: Objectives: This study aims to develop a better understanding of mothers’ knowledge, understanding, and attitude towards children’s measles immunization and explore the relationship between mothers’ understanding of measles immunization and health promotion programs in North Vietnam. Methods: Semistructured interviews were conducted with 15 mothers of children aged 1 or 6 years old between 2006 and 2010 in two provinces in North Vietnam. Ten interviews were transcribed and analysed to explore themes while other five interviews were cross-referenced for congruency. Among the ten mothers whose interviews were analysed, there were five mothers whose children received the full measles immunization schedule (two doses) and five mothers whose children received one or none of measles vaccination. Results: Mothers had different levels of understanding and a strong positive attitude towards measles immunization. Mothers considered health officers at the commune health centres who played an important role in the promotion of measles immunization, as the main source of information. The relationship between the mother’s understanding about measles immunization and health promotion programs was found to be both positive and negative. Conclusion: Mothers whose children received the full measles immunization schedule paid more attention to measles immunization and health promotion programs compared with mothers whose children received one or none of measles vaccination. Mothers’ misunderstanding about the measles immunization schedule was the main reason for choosing not to receive the measles immunizations. These findings help to improve communication with mothers about measles immunization and close the gap for 100% measles immunization in North Vietnam. Title: Analysis on Surveillance of Measles Epidemiology in Jiangxi Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: We analyzed the surveillance data of measles from January 1999 to October 2000 in Jiangxi Province for the aim of measles control. The results showed that 3?184 measles cases were reported from the province and 1 reported case died. Most of the reported measles cases were under 15 years old. The incidence of measles in age group between 7-10 years old was higher than that in other age groups and most of the reported cases having vaccination histroy were 4-10 year old children. Both the sporadic and epidemic measles in some districts existed at the same time and the reported measles cases extensively distributed in all districts of Jiangxi Province. The outbreaks of measles in some districts had affected the morbidity of measles of the whole province To prevent and control measles outbreak are the main effective measures to control this disease.

Title: RESPONS IMUN TERHADAP VIRUS CAMPAK
Abstract: Since the measles vaccine was found, the number of cases for measles infection was decreased substantially especially in the developed countries such as United States. However, measles outbreak was still frequently found in United States. This could happen as measles was still endemic in developing countries. Outbreak in US might occur as measles virus entered from outside US. To confirm this, knowledge in the molecular epidemiology for exact viral identification by molecular biology technique was necessary. Although measles virus could be assumed to be monotype, the virus could be classified into several genotypes as there was sequence difference in the 450bp of COOH-end of N gene, or sequence differences in the whole H gene. These differences showed up in several genotypes of measles virus distributing through out the globe. These genotype differences was the based of the molecular epidemiology applied for the surveillance of measles disease to eradicate measles virus in the world.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Genotype; Virology; Immunology; Virus; Measles; Measles virus; Measles vaccine; Cellular immunity; Biology; Molecular epidemiology

Title: Epidemiological analysis of measles from 1957 to 2006 in Fenghua City of Zhejiang Province
Abstract: Objective The study was conducted to analyze the epidemiological characteristics of measles in fifty years' period from 1957 to 2006 in Fenghua City for the adjustment of measles immu- nization strategy.Methods The measles epidemic in the fifty years were divided into four stages:be- fore the measles vaccination,in the measles vaccine popularization,after the measles vaccine became planned-immunization once a year and monthly measles vaccine with cold chain equipment.Geomet- ric mean was used for the average annual incidence rate at the first three stages,while arithmetic mean was used at IV stage.Results The incidence of measles and age constitution had significant differ- ences among the four stages;the onset age ranged from chiefly the early childhood and school-age to 8-month-old and over 15-year-old.Eight-month-old infants and the youth over 15-year-old ac- counted for 96.92% of the total cases in Fenghua from 2005 to 2006.Conclusion It is required to strengthen the measles vaccine immunization for junior high school students to reduce the high inci- dence of measles among older and younger people and to eradicate the measles disease.

Title: Analysis on Measles Laboratory Network Establishment and Running Situation in Hebei Province
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective The study is to evaluate the effect of measles laboratory network in speeding up measles control.Methods We analyzed the data of the measles laboratory network during 1999-2004.Results From 1999 to 2004,the measles surveillance system reported 5474 suspected measles cases.The measles laboratory network had detected 4 560 cases.The positive rate of the measles IgM antibody is 49.06%,the rubella IgM antibody is 14.78%.In 2003 and 2004,when the laboratory network received specimen and detected in three days,the ratio is 73.60%(1701/2311).During 2001-2004,the municipal measles laboratory network detected 763 blind sera specimen.The identical rate is 97.25%.During 2001-2004,after provincial measles laboratory detected 730 specimen repeatedly,the identical rate is 95.80%.During 2001～2004,checked by the national measles laboratory,the provincial laboratory got 100 marks each year.The 3 strains of wild measles virus isolated from Hebei Province are H_1 gene type. Conclusion The establishment and running of the measles laboratory network played very important role in measles surveillance system.The rate of the laboratory detected,the ratio of the specimen detected in three days and the rate of identical results are in high level,which improved the diagnosis level and quality of the suspected measles cases.The strains of the epidemic wild measles virus are H_1 gene type in the past ten years in Hebei Province.The laboratory of the measles virus isolation need be strengthened and improved.

Title: The epidemiology and clinical analysis on 150 measles cases
Source Title: Journal of Clinical and Experimental Medicine
Abstract: Objective To study Epidemiology for Measles and clinical characteristics from 2005 to 2010.Methods Clinical data of150 measles patients treated in our Hospital were analyzed retrospectively.Results 150 measles patients(male 102,female 48),The ratio of males to females was 2.13∶1,The least ages of patient was 4 months and the oldest was 43 years,all have this locality household register,the prevalent time about Measles is from late spring to early autumn.The antibody positive rate of serum Measles Virus IgM was 79.33%.Conclusion Measles patients may be closely related to Irregular vaccinating People against measles or decreased antibody titers after vaccination.Patient s were characterized by fever,skin rashes,measles membrane and so on.It is effective measure against measles through first immunization age ahead properly,intensifying vaccinating children of the right age,strengthen the health care education and early diagnosis,treatment and isolation of measles patients.

Title: Evolvement on the Definition of Measles Elimination and Verification
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Since measles elimination was first proposed before measles vaccine was licensed in the United States in 1962,definitions of measles elimination have evolved and progressed from"assuming a reduction to zero of the incidence of measles"to"the absence of endemic measles transmission",and"incidence of measles1/per million population as an operational definition".In December 2010,WHO defined measles elimination as"the absence of endemic measles transmission in a defined geographical area(e.g.region)for≥12 months under the presence of a well performing surveillance system",which emphasized the significance of well performing measles surveillance system.Measles elimination requires an objective,external verification process.The WHO Regional Offices for the Americas,Eastern Mediterranean,Europe and Western Pacific have established or are establishing processes and criteria for verification of measles elimination,which including vaccination coverage,disease incidence,virological surveillance,comprehensive surveillance quality.and sustainability of national immunization programmes.National verification committee(NVC)is necessary to be established in China to evaluate the target and indicators of measles elimination are achieved objectively and independently.
Fields of Study: Virology; Measles; Transmission (medicine); Measles vaccine; Immunization; Population; Measles elimination; Vaccination coverage; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Analysis of Masles in Huadu District from 2006 to 2008
Abstract: Objective To provide the scientific basis for eliminating measles through analysing the epidemiological characteristics of measles in Huadu District.Method Excel,Spss and epidemiological investigation were conducted on the data of measles recorded from 2006 to 2008 in HuaDu district.Results There were 559 cases of measles incidence in Huadu District from 2006 to 2008.The general trend of the measles cases was ascending.The measles cases mainly occurred in the floating population who lived between the urban and rural areas.The peak time of the incidence of measles was from April to August every year.The floating population was the main population of measles incidence.Most of the cases were non-immuse or unknown immunization.Conclusions The causes of the ascent of measles cases are the floating of temporary population and the weak immunity to measles.We should strengthen immunization planning and management and improve the level of population immunity.

Title: ANALYSIS ON THE EPIDEMIOLOGICAL CHARACTERISTICS OF MEASLES IN HAIKOU CITY FROM 2005 TO 2006
Abstract: [Objective] To study the current epidemiological characteristics and elimination strategies of measles.[Methods] Description epidemiology analysis was applied to analyze epidemic status of measles in Haikou from 2005 to 2006.[Results] The prevalence of measles continued 2 years in Haikou.The epidemic situation spread extensive and the disease cas-es scattered widely.Infectious foci of measles took turn in different districts of the city.The incidence of measles was high in the floating population,children under 2 years as well as non-immunization population.The incidence was highest for none docu-mented vaccination people.But one part of cases was vaccinated.The rural patients of measles outbreak concentrated in pupil,and urban patients of measles concentrated in floating population.[Conclusion] It's also possible that measles could spread widely in current high level of MV inoculation even though the immunization rate has already reached to a high level.The ap-propriate measures must be taken to eliminate the accumulation for measles susceptible person.

Title: Progress in implementing measles mortality reduction strategies, India 2010–2011.
Abstract: In 2005, an estimated 92,000 deaths occurred in India from measles among children aged <5 years. Estimates from 2008 indicate that 77% of global measles mortality was attributable to measles deaths in the World Health Organization (WHO) South-East Asia Region, the majority of which occurred in India. These figures highlight the importance of India in attaining regional and global measles mortality reduction targets. In 2008, the Indian National Technical Advisory Group on Immunization (NTAGI) recommended introduction of a second dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV2), delivered through routine vaccination in states with ≥80% coverage with the first dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV1), or through mass vaccination campaigns in states with <80% MCV1 coverage. Based on these recommendations, the government of India initiated MCV2 introduction in late 2010. This report provides an update on MCV1 coverage, progress in implementing MCV2, and measles outbreak surveillance activities conducted in eight states during 2006-2010. India has initiated implementation of a measles mortality reduction strategy, but the pace of implementation is variable across states. Strong national and state leadership and commitment to rapid reduction of measles mortality are essential to achieve the full benefits of this strategy.

Title: Evaluation on measles supplementary immunization activity in Tianzhu county,Guizhou
Abstract: Objective To evaluate the measles supplementary immunization activity(SIA) in Tianzhu County in 2004 and provide evidence for developing the strategies to accelerate the measles control.Methods The comprehensive analysis was conducted on the preparedness,implementation and assessment(data collecting and coverage survey) of the SIA and the incidence of measles since the implementation of SIA.Results A total of 64 782 children were reached by SIA,both reported coverage rate and surveyed coverage rate were over 95%.The incidence of measles decreased by 98.56% compared with the average annual incidence during 2000-2004.Conclusion The effect of measles SIA in Tianzhu was great.High quality measles SIA can reduce the incidence of measles quickly.

Title: A survey on measles vaccination policies in Italian Regions
Source Title: Annali di igiene : medicina preventiva e di comunita
Abstract: In this article we report the results of a survey conducted in early 2002, in order to describe the measles vaccination policies implemented in the Italian Regions. All the twenty-one Regions returned the questionnaire, which included information on measles vaccination coverage and vaccination strategies. Up to now, all the Regions provide measles vaccine free of charge, but the year of free offer introduction ranges from early 1980's to 2000. Despite measles vaccination coverage by 2 years of age is improving, none of the Regions has reached the 95% goal yet. Eighteen Regions do offer measles vaccination to older unvaccinated children, and 10 Regions actively offer a 2nd dose. Most of the Regions conducted special catch-up campaigns; nevertheless these activities took place in different years and targeted different age-groups. These results, along with the evidence of high measles incidence in Italy, underline the need of a coordinated national strategy, in order to reach measles elimination.

Title: Measles cases in tatarstan republic and the effect of revaccination of children aged 6 to 7
Abstract: <jats:p>The frequency and dynamics of measles cases in Tatarstan Republic within recent 10 years are analyzed. Its successive decrease, especially in connection with introduction in 1986 of revaccination by living measles vaccine of children aged 6 to 7. Immunization against measles performed during 199495 proved to be highly efficient: the sickness rate index 1,8 per 100 thousands of population proved to be the lowest index in a time of measles registration in the Republic. Elimination of endogenic measles in the Republic is fully actual reaching not less than 95% of immunization against measles, appropriate revealing of children falling ill and the performance of antiepidemic measles complex.</jats:p>

Title: Two measles outbreaks after importation--Utah, March-June 2011.
Source Title: MMWR. Morbidity and mortality weekly report
Publisher: Department of Health and Human Services
Abstract: Before licensure of a measles vaccine in 1963, more than 500,000 measles cases on average were reported in the United States each year during 1951-1962. By 1993, through measles vaccination and control efforts, only 312 cases were reported nationwide. In 2000, the last year in which an outbreak had occurred in Utah, measles was declared "not endemic in the United States," but measles importations continue to occur, leading to outbreaks, especially among unvaccinated persons. Many U.S. health-care personnel have never seen a measles patient, which might hamper diagnosis and delay reporting. During March-June 2011, local health departments collaborated with the state health department in Utah to investigate two measles outbreaks comprising 13 confirmed cases. The first outbreak, with seven confirmed cases, was associated with an unvaccinated U.S. resident who traveled internationally; the second, with six confirmed cases, had an undetermined source. The genotype D4 sequences obtained from these two outbreaks differed by a single nucleotide, suggesting two separate importations. Health-care providers should remind their patients of the importance of being current with measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccination; this is especially important before international travel. Measles should be considered in the differential diagnosis of febrile rash illness, especially in unvaccinated persons with recent international travel. Reporting a confirmed or suspected case immediately to public health authorities is critical to limit the spread of measles.

Title: Analysis on the Surveillance of Measles From 2001-2004 in Liaocheng City of Shandong Province,China
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To analyze the surveillance of measles from 1999-2004 in Liaocheng City of Shandong Province and provide evidence for the strategies on measles elimination in the future.Methods The surveillance of measles by the special measles surveillance system was used for the analysis of the epidemiological characteristics and its epidemic factors.Results 314 measles cases were reported with the yearly average incidence rate of 0.94/100 000 from 1999-2004 in Liaocheng City.There were significant differences of the measles incidence among counties of the City.The peak of onset was occurred from April to July.Most cases were found among children under 15 year-old.The sporadic epidemic at the city level and outbreak with 26.11% of the total measles cases in some counties existed at the same time.The pupils and college students were the high-risk age groups for the measles outbreaks.It showed that the onsets of measles were related with the low coverage rate of the routine immunization for measles vaccine and the poor quality of measles surveillance at the grass roots.Conclusions The measles surveillance system should be further improved especially for the surveillance quality at the grass roots.To get the high coverage rate of measles vaccine and accelerate measles control,the EPI routine immunization in some weak and hard areas should be enhanced and the supplemental immunization activities of measles vaccine should be availably carried out at the suitable time.

Title: Measles vaccines
Publisher: Panstwowy Zaklad Higieny/National Institute of Hygiene
Abstract: The World Health Organization has settled the goal of measles elimination in European Region by the year 2007. The proposed measles elimination strategy is to reduce an estimated proportion of susceptible individuals in the population to low levels by intensive vaccination and to maintain these low levels for some years. In this strategy, the quality of measles vaccine used for immunization is crucial. The different kinds of measles vaccines used at present in the world and novel generations of vaccines are presented.

Title: Diagnóstico del sarampión según la definición de casos clínicos en zonas donde el dengui es endémico: repercusiones para la vigilancia y el control del sarmpiónl
Abstract: Diagnosis of measles by clinical case definition in dengue-endemic areas: implications for measles surveillance and contml In many countries, measles surveillance relies heavily on the use of a standard clinical case definition; however, the clinical signs and symptoms of measles are similar to those of dengue. For example, during 1985, in Puerto Rico, 22 (23%) of 94 cases of illnesses with rashes that met the measles cMcal case definition were serologicauy confirmed as measles, but 32 (34%) others were serologically confirmed as dengue. Retrospective analysis at the San Juan Laboratories of the Centers for Disease Control showed also that at least 28% of aU laboratory-con6rmed cases of dengue in Puerto Rico in 1985 met the measles clinical case definition. If the true measles vaccine efficacy (VE) is assumed to be 90%, the occurrence of Iaboratory-confirmed dengue cases that meet the measles clinical case defkition results in a reduction of the apparent measles VE to only 64% (a 29% relative reduction from the true VE). The results of the study demonstrate the importance of a laboratory-based surveillance system in measles control or elimination efforts in dengue-endemic areas.

Title: Analysis on Epidemiological Characteristics of Measles in Anyang 2008 and Effect of Supplementary immunization activities
Abstract: Objective To know epidemiological characteristics of measles in Anyang 2008 and effect of supplementary immunization activities,provide evidence for measures prevention and control.Methods Analyzing epidemiological characteristics of measles in Anyang 2008 and effect of supplementary immunization activities in January 2009.Results The measles incindence in 2008 was 16.60 cases per 100000 population.The measles cases aged 10 years accounted for 84.11%.The measles cases aged 2 years accounted for 54.86%.54.75% of the cases aged 8 months～10 years were not vaccinated.Supplementary immunization activities,vaccinated 301389,The rate of vaccination was 100%.Conclusion The increaser peoples without immunization were primary reason of measles prevalence.Keep high immune basis vaccine be inoculated rate,keep high immune success rate,Supplementary immunization activities at suitable time,and sensitive measles supervise system were primary measure of accelerate measles control.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Immunization; Medicine; Environmental health

Title: ANALYSIS ON EPIDEMIOLOGY OF MEASLES IN YIBIN DURING 2004.
Abstract: Objective:The epidemiological characters and tendency of measles are mastered by analyzing the incidence of inhabitants in yibin,in order to provide gist for measles control and prevention.Methods:Measles cases were collected in yibin during 2004 and epidemiological features were analyzed.Results:The results showed that 229 measles cases were reported in yibin during 2004,the incidence of measles increaed.The highest incidence of measles(26.67/100 000)was in changling.The high incidence period was from March to February.The all inoculation of measles'vaccination was low.Conclusion:It is necessary to monitor more measles cases in the future.More training class of measles vaccination procedure should be done more scientifically.At present it is our most important task to carry out health education and propaganda.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Health education; Virology; Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; High incidence; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: Evaluation on effect of the two round supplementary immunization of measles vaccine in Baoshan of Yunnan province
Abstract: Objective To evaluate effect of two round supplementary immunization of measles vaccine in Baoshan of Yunnan province.Methods Descriptive analysis was made according to the vaccination rate of measles vaccine for supplementary immunization.Data were obtained from the field investigation and the legal measles epidemic reporting system,and detection of antibody levels.Results The two round vaccination rate of measles vaccine was all above 98%.At the same time,the antibody level of measles was greatly improved to 95.02%(P0.001)and the incidence of measles greatly decreased from 5.99/100000 to 1.08/100000 of 2009 and 0.12/100000 of 2010,finally there was no measles cases which had been reported so far.Conclusions The incidence of measles was effectively controlled after two round supplementary immunization of measles vaccine.
Fields of Study: Virology; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Vaccination rate; Reporting system; Antibody level; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine
