Title: Analysis on Immunization Coverage Rate of Measles Vaccine and Measles Incidence in Gaozhou
Source Title: International Medicine and Health Guidance News
Abstract: Objective To probe into the relationship between immunization coverage rate of measles vaccine and the measles incidence,to provide scientific evidence for measles control further. Methods We got the measles coverage rate and measles incidence by routine coverage rate reporting system and notifiable disease reporting system,and analized them statisticaly. Results After the measles vaccination had been introduced into EPI,the measles incidence of Gaozhou come down from 300per100000 to 50per100000,the dropping down range is 83.3 percent.And with the coverage rate of measles vaccine going up the measles incidence appeared downtrend increasingly.The measles incidence dropping off range is much greater than the going up range of the coverage rate,since the greatest dropping off range is 90.29percent,the going up range is only about 2 percent.Measles incidence was negatively correlated with the coverage rate of measles vaccine and there was statistic significance. (r=0.9010,p0.01) Conclusion As the coverage rate of measles vaccine promoting ,the measles incidence decreased year by year,and because of the effect of immunity protective screen it can achieve the goal of measles control completely or elinmination of measles without have 100 percent of coverage rate of measles vaccine.
Fields of Study: Virology; Pediatrics; Vaccination; Measles; Notifiable disease; Measles vaccine; Reporting system; Vaccination coverage; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine

Title: An Epidemiological Study on Measles Cases with Vaccination History of Measles Attenuated Live Vaccine
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Vaccines and Immunization
Abstract: Objective To know the distribution characteristics of measles cases with vaccination history of measles attenuated live vaccine(MV)and to provide base data for Measles control and prevention.Methods A descriptive epidemiological study was conducted to analyze measles cases with or without vaccination history of measles attenuated vaccine reported in Zhejiang province in 2009.Results The confirmed measles cases with vaccination history of measles vaccine was obviously lower than(16.44%)non-measles cases(41.45%).The shortest interval between the latest MV vaccination and onset of measles case was 0d,the longest is 25 years,with a median of 3 months.The occurrence of Measles of interval proportions 1 month,1-11 months,1-9 years,10-25 years were 42.10%,30.19%,19.50%,6.92% respectively.The interval distribution was different by age groups.Some symptoms were milder in the cases with MV history than that without MV history.For cases with different interval between recent MV vaccination and onset of measles,conjunctivitis was less in those of 6-10d than others.Conclusion Measles cases with MV history could be resulted from primary or secondary immunization failure,or time vaccinated at the late incubation period.Few cases may actually be vaccine reactions at 6-10d after vaccination.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Pediatrics; Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Vaccine failure; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Attenuated Live Vaccine; Attenuated vaccine; Medicine

Title: Seroprotective of measles antibody in 4-year-old children who previously had received single doses of measles vaccine or measles – mumps – rubella vaccines at 9–12 months old
Abstract: Objective : To study seroprotective rate of measles antibody in 4-year-old children who previously had received single doses of measles vaccine or measles – mumps – rubella vaccines at 9–12 months old : To compare seroprotective rate of measles antibody between 4–year-old children who previously had received single doses of measles vaccine and received measles – mumps – rubella vaccines at 9–12 months old Methods : A cross sectional serological study in 4 –year-old children who previously had received single doses of measles vaccine or measles – mumps – rubella vaccines at 9–12 months old in Kleang district from June 2112 to August 2112 : Measles antibody were measured by ELISA assays at the Department of Microbiology, Faculty of Medicine Chulalongkorn University , Thailand Results : 108 children were included in the study, 56 male and 52 female. Average age 55 months SD =3.396 : The subjects were divided into two groups, 1) 73 children received only measles vaccine and 2) 35 children received measles-mumps-rubella vaccines : 58 Children ( 53.7 percent) had protective measles antibody , 50 Children (46.3 percent) had low measles antibody level that not enough for protect measles infection : Mean of measles antibody level 1.154 IU /ml,SD=1.131 : Range of measles antibody level is 0.06 – 5.42 IU /ml Conclusion : 58 children ( 53.7 percent) in this study had protective measles antibody and the other had low measles antibody level that not enough for protect measles infection.
Fields of Study: Pediatrics; Immunology; Measles; Rubella; Measles vaccine; Measles antibody; Measles antibody level; Mumps measles rubella; Serology; Medicine

Title: Quantifying the impact of the measles vaccine by estimating the number of measles cases prevented for each state from 1964 to 2010
Abstract: Measles is regarded as one of the most contagious infectious diseases, but since the licensure of the vaccine in 1963, the incidence of measles has decreased significantly. Although the United States achieved measles elimination in 2000, there are still outbreaks due to pockets of low vaccination coverage and imported cases. This essay will provide background information about the virus, the disease, the vaccine, and current trends in measles vaccination. The objective of the analysis in this essay was to estimate, at the state level, the number of measles cases prevented since the licensure of the vaccine.; ; Using data from before the vaccine was licensed, 20 statistical models were assessed based on their predictive ability, with the best model being used to estimate the number of measles cases there would have been without the vaccine. The different approaches examined were descriptive statistics, linear regression, moving averages, and time series models. The estimated number of measles cases from 1964-2010 were compared to the observed number of measles cases to estimate the number of measles cases prevented.; ; The best fit model estimated that the total number of measles cases prevented from 1964-2010 in the United States was over 23 million, while the number of cases prevented at the state level ranged from 4.1 million to only 155.; ; The best predictive statistical model demonstrated that the measles vaccine prevented millions of cases across the United States and that the impact of the vaccine varied among the states. The public health significance of this analysis is that it adds to the analytical evidence demonstrating the significant effect that the measles vaccine had in preventing the disease, which can be used to support the recommendations for high coverage rates of vaccinations or to support the widespread implementation of new vaccines.
Fields of Study: Outbreak; Public health; Virology; Vaccination; Measles; Disease; Measles vaccine; Licensure; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Analysis of Influencing Factors for Measles in Dezhou City in 2008
Abstract: [Objective]To investigate the influencing factors of measles in Dezhou so as to provide the strategies for measles control.[Methods]A 1∶1 matched case-control study was conducted with 104 cases of measles.Multivariate conditional logistic analysis was used for data analysis.[Results]The simple factor analysis showed that the risk of measles was related to hospital exposure,contact with patients,outgoing;measles vaccine immunization was a protective factor.Multivariate conditional logistic analysis showed that OR of hospital exposure was 7.19;OR of contact with patients was 5.21;OR of measles vaccine immunization was 0.15.[Conclusion]Hospital exposure,contact with patients and no measles vaccine immunization make a person liable to measles.
Fields of Study: Pediatrics; Immunology; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Logistic analysis; Medicine

Title: Analysis on Epidemiological Characteristics of Measles from 2004 to 2008 in Anhui Province
Abstract: Objective To analyze the epidemiological characteristics of measles from 2004 to 2008 in Anhui province and provide evidence for developing measures of measles elimination.Methods Data of measles were analyzed by descriptive epidemiological method.Results The annual incidence of measles was 5.59/lakh from 2004 to 2008.Lower age group was the main target of Measles,The proportions of measles cases aged below 1 year increased year by year,3～6 months were the peak ages.According to the analysis of vaccine immunization history of measles cases in 2008,27.96% had measles vaccine immunization history,45.35% were not immunized,and 26.69% had unknown inoculation history.Conclusion Completing the vaccination rates of 2 MV doses and the timely one of the first dose,enhancing the measles surveillance,preventing the in-hospital infection,controlling the onset of measles in higher age group,conducting supplementary immunization and subsequent intensified immunization were all the keys to control and eliminate measles.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Pediatrics; Immunology; Vaccination; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Immunization history; Annual incidence; Medicine

Title: The laboratory test of measles diganosis
Source Title: Chinese Journal of Health Laboratory Technology
Abstract: Objective:To find out the functions of the IgM and IgG antibody in diagnosing measles.Methods:To detect special IgM and IgG antibody of serum samples of suspicious measles patients with the testing kits,and cultivate the virus in pharynx mop samples of the suspicious measles patients.Results:With the method of detecting IgG antibody of double samples of one patient,in one measles breaking out spot,11 of 22 suspicious measles patients were diagnosed.Out of the 11 measles patients,5 persons had the measles IgM antibody in blood,and the measles virus was cultivated in pharynx mop samples of 2 persons(one of them,measles IgM antibody of blood sample was negative).Conclusion:With the result of IgM detecting,the rate of omitted diagnose for measles might be very high,but with the result of virus cultivating,the rate of omitted diagnose for measles might be much higher.The available method for diagnosing measles could be detecting IgG antibody in the blood of acute and getting back phases.
Fields of Study: Virology; Antibody; Immunology; Virus; Measles; Pharynx; Measles virus; Measles IgM Antibody; Serum samples; Laboratory test; Medicine

Title: Analysis of Meales Monitoring in Ningxia from 2005 to 2010
Abstract: Objective To understand the epidemic feature of measles in Ningxia and to explore the control and elimination of measles in strategy.Methods Descriptive epidemiological methods were used to analyze the epidemiological characteristics of measles in Ningxia from 2005 to 2010,laboratory test results,control measures.Surveillance of measles case information from the the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region Center for Disease Control and prevention of measles epidemic surveillance system.Results The report incidence of measles in 2005 was 38.2 /100000 and it was 1.42 /100000 in 2010;the distributio of measles showed seasonal and had a peak from March to June;Babies under one year of age and the crowd 20 years old or above were the major age groups for measles incidence.Conclusion Measles vaccination rate needs to further be improved and the monitoring measures needs to be strengthened for control and elimination of measles.
Fields of Study: Epidemiology; Epidemiological method; Immunology; Measles; Age groups; Disease control; Vaccination rate; Laboratory test; Incidence (epidemiology); Medicine; Environmental health

Title: Results analysis of measles supplementary immunization in children under 15 years old in Qinghai province
Abstract: objectiveTo analyze the Measles supplementaryimmunization activities in Qinghai province to reduce measles cases in a short time,and make a plan to eleminate measles.The field investigation reports during measles supplementaryimmunization activities,assessment report and National communicablediseases reporting system were comprehensively analyzed.Totally of 1100315 children were immunized,both reported and survey coverage were over 95%.Measles cases decreased remarkably after measles supplementaryimmunization,with one-third of the reported cases are rubella.according to the reported measles datas of July-December 2006,88% of the reported cases are children aged 8month-14 years old,32% had no immunization record or not clear.High qualified measles supplementaryimmunization can remarkably reduce measles cases in a short time,target children need to be exact,and a full coverageto get a protective wall against measles virus.The coverage must reach over 95%,and get prepared to the AEFIs.follow-up campaignafter 3-4 years shouldbe considered and improve routine coverage in Qinghai province.
Fields of Study: Pediatrics; Immunology; Measles; Measles vaccine; Immunization; Immunization record; Reporting system; Medicine

Title: Measles is back
Abstract: Measles is one of the most deadly vaccine preventable diseases. The incidence of measles, and resultant mortality, had dropped drastically following the introduction of widespread measles immunisation since the 1960s. However, there is currently a worldwide surge in measles cases, with a marked increase over the past 3 years. Measles outbreaks and endemic transmission have been re-established in countries which had previously achieved measles elimination. The rise in measles cases has been mainly attributed to a drop in the recommended two dose vaccination schedule below the 95% uptake threshold necessary for interruption of transmission and sustainment of herd protection. This resurgence of measles is largely a result of the damage done by Andrew Wakefield, who in 1998 incorrectly and maliciously suggested a possible link between the measles, mumps and rubella (MMR) vaccine and autism. Such a possible association has subsequently been disproven by several scientifically robust studies. Still, most cases of measles have occurred in unimmunised individuals, mainly teenagers, who had missed out on vaccination in early childhood, and in infants under one year of age, who are too young to be vaccinated. Measles is highly contagious, with up to 18 people being potentially infected from a single case, so containment measures are important to prevent spread. These include isolation and immediate notification of suspected or confirmed cases, as well as wearing appropriate personal protective equipment when in contact with these patients. Health care professionals have a crucial role in promoting measles immunisation, which is the only rational way of preventing measles.
